Item 1. Business
This Annual Report on Form 10-K and the documents incorporated herein by reference contain forward-looking statements based on expectations, estimates, and projections as of the date of this filing. Actual results and outcomes may differ materially from those expressed in forward-looking statements. See Item 1A of Part I — "Risk Factors." As used herein, "Amazon.com," "we," "our," and similar terms include Amazon.com, Inc. and its subsidiaries, unless the context indicates otherwise.
General
We seek to be Earth's most customer-centric company. We are guided by four principles: customer obsession rather than competitor focus, passion for invention, commitment to operational excellence, and long-term thinking. In each of our segments, we serve our primary customer sets, consisting of consumers, sellers, developers, enterprises, content creators, advertisers, and employees.
We have organized our operations into three segments: North America, International, and Amazon Web Services ("AWS"). These segments reflect the way the Company evaluates its business performance and manages its operations. Information on our net sales is contained in Item 8 of Part II, "Financial Statements and Supplementary Data — Note 10 — Segment Information."
Consumers
We serve consumers through our online and physical stores and focus on selection, price, and convenience. We design our stores to enable hundreds of millions of unique products to be sold by us and by third parties across dozens of product categories. Customers access our offerings through our websites, mobile apps, Alexa, devices, streaming, and physically visiting our stores. We also manufacture and sell electronic devices, including Kindle, Fire tablet, Fire TV, Echo, Ring, Blink, and eero, and we develop and produce media content. We seek to offer our customers low prices, fast and free delivery, easy-to-use functionality, a
…[truncated 3236 chars]…
in digital or other formats. We believe that the principal competitive factors in our retail businesses include selection, price, and convenience, including fast and reliable fulfillment. Additional competitive factors for our seller and enterprise services include the quality, speed, and reliability of our services and tools, as well as customers' ability and willingness to change business practices. Some of our current and potential competitors have greater resources, longer histories, more customers, greater brand recognition, and greater control over inputs critical to our various businesses. They may secure better terms from suppliers, adopt more aggressive pricing, pursue restrictive distribution agreements that restrict our access to supply, direct consumers to their own offerings instead of ours, lock-in potential customers with restrictive terms, and devote more resources to technology, infrastructure, fulfillment, and marketing. The Internet facilitates competitive entry and comparison shopping, which enhances the ability of new, smaller, or lesser-known businesses to compete against us. Each of our businesses is also subject to rapid change and the development of new business models and the entry of new and well-funded competitors. Other companies also may enter into business combinations or alliances that strengthen their competitive positions.
Intellectual Property
We regard our trademarks, service marks, copyrights, patents, domain names, trade dress, trade secrets, proprietary technologies, and similar intellectual property as critical to our success, and we rely on trademark, copyright, and patent law, trade-secret protection, and confidentiality and/or license agreements with our employees, customers, partners, and others to protect our proprietary rights. We have registered, or applied for the registration of, a number of U.S. and international domain names, trademarks, service marks, and copyrights. Additionally, we have filed U.S. and international patent applications covering certain of our proprietary technology.
Seasonality
Our business is affected by seasonality, which historically has resulted in higher sales volume during our fourth quarter, which ends December 31.
Human Capital
Our employees are critical to our mission of being Earth's most customer-centric company. As of December 31, 2022, we employed approximately 1,541,000 full-time and part-time employees. Additionally, we use independent contractors and temporary personnel to supplement our workforce. Competition for qualified personnel is intense, particularly for software engineers, computer scientists, and other technical staff, and constrained labor markets have increased competition for personnel across other parts of our business.
As we strive to be Earth's best employer, we focus on investment and innovation, inclusion and diversity, safety, and engagement to hire and develop the best talent. We rely on numerous and evolving initiatives to implement these objectives and invent mechanisms for talent development, including competitive pay and benefits, flexible work arrangements, and skills training and educational programs such as Amazon Career Choice (funded education for hourly employees) and the Amazon Technical Academy (software development engineer training). Over 100,000 Amazon employees around the world have participated in Career Choice. We also continue to inspect and refine the mechanisms we use to hire, develop, evaluate, and retain our employees to promote equity for all candidates and employees. In addition, safety is integral to everything we do at Amazon and we continue to invest in safety improvements such as capital improvements, new safety technology, vehicle safety controls, and engineering ergonomic solutions. Our safety team is dedicated to using the science of safety to solve complex problems and establish new industry best practices. We also provide mentorship and support resources to our employees, and have deployed numerous programs that advance employee engagement, communication, and feedback.
4
Table of Contents
Available Information
Our investor relations website is amazon.com/ir and we encourage investors to use it as a way of easily finding information about us. We promptly make available on this website, free of charge, the reports that we file or furnish with the Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC"), corporate governance information (including our Code of Business Conduct and Ethics), and select press releases.
Executive Officers and Directors
The following tables set forth certain information regarding our Executive Officers and Directors as of January 25, 2023:
Information About Our Executive Officers

Jeffrey P. Bezos.
Mr. Bezos founded Amazon.com in 1994 and has served as Executive Chair since July 2021. He has served as Chair of the Board since 1994 and served as Chief Executive Officer from May 1996 until July 2021, and as President from 1994 until June 1999 and again from October 2000 to July 2021.

Mr. Jassy has served as President and Chief Executive Officer since July 2021, CEO Amazon Web Services from April 2016 until July 2021, and Senior Vice President, Amazon Web Services, from April 2006 until April 2016.

AMZN.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: AMZN (as of 2024-07-01)**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
*   **Revenue & Earnings:** TTM revenue is $590.74B. Q1 2024 revenue of $143.31B shows +12.5% YoY growth but a -15.7% QoQ decline from the seasonally strong Q4 2023 ($169.96B). TTM net income is $37.68B. Q1 2024 EPS of $0.98 is +216.1% YoY but -3.0% QoQ.
*   **Margins:** Margins are expanding. Q1 2024 gross margin is 49.3% ($70.68B / $143.31B), up from 46.8% a year prior. Q1 2024 operating margin is 10.7% ($15.31B / $143.31B), up from 3.7% in Q1 2023. TTM operating income is $47.38B.
*   **Cash Flow:** Cash flow statement data is unavailable for all periods provided.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total equity has grown from $131.40B (Q2 2022) to $216.66B (Q1 2024). Total debt is stable at $66.85B. Total assets stand at $530.97B.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** The significant YoY improvement in net margin (from 2.5% in Q1 2023 to 7.3% in Q1 2024) and ROE (from 2.8% in July 2023 to 17.4% in June 2024) indicates improved operational efficiency. The trajectory from negative EPS (-$0.20 in Q2 2022) to consistent positive earnings supports this.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The debt-to-equity ratio is 0.309. Total equity ($216.66B) substantially exceeds total debt ($66.85B). The equity base has grown consistently over the last eight quarters.

**3. Valuation vs. History**
*   The current P/E (diluted) is 54.72. This is at the lower end of its 365-day range of 50.0 to 326.2, having compressed significantly from 313.78 in July 2023.
*   The current P/B ratio is 9.52, near the top of its 365-day range of 7.4 to 9.8.
*   The current earnings yield is 1.8%, and the net margin is 6.4%, both at the high end of their respective 365-day ranges (net margin range: 0.0 to 0.1).

**4. Price Level & Return Context**
*   **Price Level:** The latest close is $193.25, near the 365-day high of $199.84 and well above the low of $118.35.
*   **Returns:** The stock has returned +49.8% over the past year and +7.1% over the last quarter. The quarterly path shows strong momentum: +21.7% (Q3 2023), +17.6% (Q4 2023), +7.1% (Q1 2024).
*   **Fundamental vs. Price Context:** The +49.8% annual price return has occurred alongside a fundamental shift from a P/E of 313.78 to 54.72, driven by a 216.1% YoY increase in Q1 EPS. The recent +7.1% quarterly price gain followed a Q1 2024 with a -15.7% QoQ revenue decline.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** Continued margin expansion is the clearest quantitative upside. Operating margin improved from 3.7% (Q1 2023) to 10.7% (Q1 2024). If sustained, this drives significant earnings growth even with moderate revenue growth.
*   **Downside:** The primary quantitative downside is valuation compression risk. The P/E of 54.72 remains elevated, and the P/B of 9.52 is at a cycle high. A slowdown in the rate of margin improvement or revenue growth could pressure the multiple.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The fundamental trajectory shows strong earnings and margin recovery, which has been the primary driver of the stock's re-rating from a P/E of 313.78 to 54.72. The balance sheet is resilient. However, the current valuation (P/E 54.72, P/B 9.52) prices in a significant portion of this improvement. The recent price momentum (+49.8% annual) has outpaced the most recent quarterly fundamental trend (QoQ revenue decline). The risk/reward appears balanced to moderately unfavorable at current levels, contingent on the continued, rapid expansion of margins from already improved levels.
AMZN.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN)**
**Decision Date: 2024-07-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from 10-K, 2023-02-03)**
Amazon operates as a customer-centric company across three segments: North America, International, and Amazon Web Services (AWS). Its core businesses include:
*   **E-commerce & Physical Retail:** Serving consumers and third-party sellers through online/physical stores, focusing on selection, price, and convenience. This includes fulfillment, subscription services (Prime), and device manufacturing (Kindle, Echo, etc.).
*   **Cloud Computing (AWS):** Providing on-demand technology services (compute, storage, database, AI/ML) to developers, enterprises, and governments.
*   **Advertising:** Offering sponsored ads, display, and video advertising services to sellers, vendors, and publishers.
*   **Content & Services:** Enabling content creators and offering digital streaming. The business is highly competitive across all segments, facing rivals from retail, technology, logistics, and advertising.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated, from supplied text)**
*   **Growth Catalysts & Outlook:**
    *   **2024-06-01:** Amazon provided an "optimistic Q2 outlook, above expectations," with projected net sales growth of 7-11% to $144-149 billion, based on robust Q1 performance.
    *   **2024-06-04 & 2024-05-21:** Analysis highlights AWS and digital advertising as key growth drivers. The advertising segment is noted to be approaching a $60 billion annual run rate, with combined AWS and advertising revenue estimated to reach a $160 billion run rate by year-end 2024.
    *   **2024-06-29:** News articles identify Amazon as a beneficiary of artificial intelligence (AI) growth.
*   **Competitive Position:**
    *   **2024-06-29:** A consumer review concluded that the discount platform Temu is "not a big threat to Amazon," citing a return to Amazon for purchases.
*   **Market Sentiment:**
    *   **2024-06-28 to 2024-06-30:** Multiple financial news articles and analyses recommend Amazon as a top stock to buy, citing its growth potential and solid performance.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Regulatory & Legal Risk (Confirmed):**
    *   **2024-06-17:** The U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) filed a lawsuit against Amazon. The suit is described as potentially "ripping apart" the business and harming consumers and small businesses.
    *   **2024-06-10:** UK retailers filed a £1 billion lawsuit against Amazon, claiming data misuse and alleging non-negotiable 30% commissions.
    *   **2024-05-15:** The Arizona Attorney General accused Amazon of being a monopoly and deceiving consumers regarding its "Buy Box" and Fulfillment By Amazon (FBA) program.
    *   **2024-05-21:** A class-action lawsuit alleged Amazon unlawfully used facial recognition technology in violation of Illinois' Biometric Information Privacy Act.
    *   **Offset:** The 10-K (2023-02-03) acknowledges intense competition and the risk of new business models, but does not specifically address these later lawsuits. The company's scale and resources may be used to contest these actions.
*   **Competitive Risk (Confirmed):**
    *   **10-K (2023-02-03):** The filing details a vast and intensely competitive landscape, noting that some competitors have greater resources, longer histories, and may secure better supplier terms or pursue restrictive agreements.
    *   **Offset:** Recent news (2024-06-29) suggests current discount competitors like Temu are not perceived as a major threat by some consumers. The company's integrated ecosystem (Prime, AWS, Ads) is a structural advantage.

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral.** The narrative presents a strong, diversified franchise with confirmed near-term growth catalysts in AWS and advertising, supported by an optimistic Q2 2024 guidance. However, this is materially offset by a significant and recent escalation in regulatory and legal risks, including major lawsuits from the FTC and state attorneys general that directly challenge core business practices. The balance between powerful growth drivers and substantial, unresolved legal threats results in a neutral qualitative outlook as of the decision date.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Amazon's fundamental inflection is real and durable—operating margin expanded from 3.7% to 10.7% YoY in Q1 2024, TTM net income hit $37.68B, and ROE surged to 17.4%—but the stock at a P/E of 54.72 and near its 365-day high prices in much of this improvement, leaving moderate upside rather than a clear bargain.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Amazon's fundamental inflection is real and durable—operating margin expanded from 3.7% to 10.7% YoY in Q1 2024, TTM net income hit $37.68B, and ROE surged to 17.4%—but the stock at a P/E of 54.72 and near its 365-day high prices in much of this improvement, leaving moderate upside rather than a clear bargain.
key_factors: Q1 2024 operating margin expanded to 10.7% from 3.7% YoY and gross margin to 49.3% from 46.8%, with TTM operating income of $47.38B, demonstrating structural profitability improvement across retail and AWS.
TTM net income of $37.68B and ROE of 17.4% (up from 2.8% in July 2023) confirm the earnings inflection is sustained, with EPS of $0.98 in Q1 2024 up 216.1% YoY.

Current P/E of 54.72 is near the low end of its 365-day range (50.0–326.2) but P/B of 9.52 is at a cycle high, and the stock is near its 365-day high of $199.84 after +49.8% annual return, leaving limited margin of safety.

AWS and advertising represent durable growth engines—advertising approaching a $60B annual run rate per analyst estimates, with combined AWS and advertising revenue estimated at $160B by year-end 2024—though escalating FTC and state-level regulatory lawsuits pose material downside risk to the franchise.

ITEM 1.  BUSINESS
In this report, the terms "The Coca-Cola Company," "Company," "we," "us" and "our" mean The Coca-Cola Company and all entities included in our consolidated financial statements.
General
The Coca-Cola Company is a total beverage company, and beverage products bearing our trademarks, sold in the United States since 1886, are now sold in more than 200 countries and territories. We own or license and market numerous beverage brands, which we group into the following categories: Trademark Coca-Cola; sparkling flavors; water, sports, coffee and tea; juice, value-added dairy and plant-based beverages; and emerging beverages. We own and market five of the world's top six nonalcoholic sparkling soft drink brands: Coca-Cola, Sprite, Fanta, Coca-Cola Zero Sugar and Diet Coke/Coca-Cola Light.
We make our branded beverage products available to consumers throughout the world through our network of independent bottling partners, distributors, wholesalers and retailers as well as our consolidated bottling and distribution operations. Beverages bearing trademarks owned by or licensed to the Company account for 2.2 billion of the estimated 64 billion servings of all beverages consumed worldwide every day.
We believe our success depends on our ability to connect with consumers by providing them with a wide variety of beverage options to meet their desires, needs and lifestyles. Our success further depends on the ability of our people to execute effectively, every day.
We are guided by our purpose, which is to refresh the world and make a difference. Our vision for growth has three connected pillars:
- Loved Brands. We craft meaningful brands and a choice of drinks that people love and enjoy and that refresh them in body and spirit.
- Done Sustainably. We grow our business in ways that achieve positive ch
…[truncated 20183 chars]…
djusted periodically by the Company, up to a maximum indexed ceiling price, and is adjusted quarterly based upon changes in certain sugar or sweetener prices, as applicable. The U.S. unit case volume prepared, packaged, sold and distributed under these legacy bottler's agreements is not material.
Under the terms of the bottler's agreements, bottlers in the United States generally are not authorized to manufacture fountain syrups. Rather, the Company manufactures and sells fountain syrups to authorized fountain wholesalers (including certain authorized bottlers) and some fountain retailers. These wholesalers in turn sell the syrups, or deliver them on our behalf, to restaurants and other retailers.
6
Promotional and Marketing Programs
In addition to conducting our own independent advertising and marketing activities, we may provide promotional and marketing support and/or funds to our bottlers. In most cases, we do this on a discretionary basis under the terms of commitment letters or agreements, even though we are not obligated to do so under the terms of the bottler's agreements between our Company and the bottlers. Also, on a discretionary basis in most cases, our Company may develop and introduce new products, packages and equipment to assist the bottlers. Likewise, in many instances, we provide promotional and marketing support and/or funds and/or dispensing equipment and repair services to fountain and bottle/can retailers, typically pursuant to marketing agreements. The aggregate amount provided by our Company to bottlers, resellers and other customers  of our Company's products, principally for participation in promotional and marketing programs, was $4.8 billion in 2022.
Investments in Bottling Operations
Most of our branded beverage products are prepared, packaged, distributed and sold by independent bottling partners. However, from time to time we acquire or take control of a bottling operation, often in underperforming markets where we believe we can use our resources and expertise to improve performance. Owning a bottling operation enables us to compensate for limited local resources; help focus the bottler's sales and marketing programs; assist in the development of the bottler's business and information systems; and establish an appropriate capital structure for the bottler. In line with our long-term bottling strategy, we may periodically consider options for divesting or reducing our ownership interest in a consolidated bottling operation, typically by selling all or a portion of our interest in the bottling operation to an independent bottler to improve Coca-Cola system efficiency. When we sell a consolidated bottling operation to an independent bottling partner in which we have an equity method investment, our Company continues to participate in the bottler's results of operations through our share of the equity method investee's earnings or losses.
In addition, from time to time we make equity investments representing noncontrolling interests in certain bottling operations with the intention of maximizing the strength and efficiency of the Coca-Cola system's production, marketing, sales and distribution capabilities around the world by providing expertise and resources to strengthen those businesses. These investments are intended to result in increases in unit case volume, net operating revenues and profits at the bottler level, which in turn generate increased sales for our Company's concentrate operations. When our equity investment provides us with the ability to exercise significant influence over the investee bottler's operating and financial policies, we account for the investment under the equity method.
Seasonality
Sales of our ready-to-drink beverages are somewhat seasonal, with the second and third calendar quarters historically accounting for the highest sales volumes. The volume of sales in the beverage business may be affected by weather conditions.
Competition
The commercial beverage industry is highly competitive and consists of numerous companies, ranging from small or emerging to very large and well established. These include companies that, like our Company, compete globally in multiple geographic areas, as well as businesses that are primarily regional or local in operation. Competitive products include numerous nonalcoholic sparkling soft drinks; water products, including flavored and enhanced waters; juices, juice drinks and nectars; dilutables (including syrups and powders); coffees; teas; energy drinks; sports drinks; milk and other dairy-based drinks; plant-based beverages; functional beverages, including vitamin-based products and relaxation beverages; and various other nonalcoholic beverages. These competitive products are sold to consumers in both ready-to-drink and non-ready-to-drink form. The Company has directly entered the alcohol beverage category in numerous markets outside the United States. In the United States, the Company has authorized alcohol-licensed third parties to use certain of our trademarks and related intellectual property on alcohol beverages that contain Company beverage bases. Competitive products include all flavored alcohol beverages containing various alcohol bases. In many of the countries in which we do business, PepsiCo, Inc. is a primary competitor. Other significant competitors include, but are not limited to, Nestlé S.A., Keurig Dr Pepper Inc., Danone S.A., Suntory Beverage & Food Limited, Unilever, AB InBev, Kirin Holdings, Heineken N.V., Diageo and Red Bull GmbH. We also compete against numerous regional and local companies and, increasingly, against smaller companies that are developing microbrands and selling them directly to consumers through e-commerce retailers and other e-commerce platforms. In addition, in some markets, we compete against retailers that have developed their own store or private-label beverage brands.
Competitive factors impacting our business include, but are not limited to, pricing, advertising, sales promotion programs,     in-store displays and point-of-sale marketing, digital marketing, product and ingredient innovation, increased efficiency in production techniques, the introduction of new packaging as well as new vending and dispensing equipment, contracting with marketing assets (theaters, sports arenas, universities, etc.), and brand and trademark development and protection.
Our competitive strengths include leading brands with high levels of consumer recognition and loyalty; a worldwide network of bottlers and distributors of Company products; sophisticated marketing capabilities; and a talented group of dedicated employees. Our competitive challenges include strong competitors in all geographic regions; in many countries, a concentrated

retail sector with powerful buyers able to freely choose among Company products, products of competitive beverage suppliers and individual retailers' own store or private-label beverage brands; new industry entrants; and dramatic shifts in consumer shopping methods and patterns due to a rapidly evolving digital landscape.

Water is a main ingredient in substantially all of our products. ### Quantitative Report: KO (Decision Date: 2024-07-01)

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
- **Revenue & Earnings:** TTM revenue is $46.07B. Latest Q1 2024 revenue of $11.30B shows modest YoY growth of +2.9%. TTM net income is $10.78B. Q1 2024 EPS diluted of $0.74 is up +2.8% YoY, but only +60.9% QoQ from a weak Q4 2023 ($0.46). Quarterly revenue and earnings show volatility (range: $10.12B to $11.97B revenue, $0.44 to $0.74 EPS over 8 quarters).
- **Margins:** TTM gross profit margin is ~60.0% (from ratios). Q1 2024 gross margin is 62.6% ($7.07B/$11.30B). TTM operating income is $10.09B. Q1 2024 operating margin is 18.9% ($2.14B/$11.30B), lower than Q1 2023's 30.7% ($3.37B/$10.98B).
- **Cash-Flow:** Cash flow data is explicitly unavailable for all periods.
- **Balance Sheet:** Total assets grew to $99.39B. Total equity increased to $27.95B. Total debt decreased slightly to $36.50B from $37.51B in Q4 2023. Cash increased to $15.20B from $12.36B.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality:** Net margin has been stable between 20% and 24% over the 8-quarter window (range from statements). However, QoQ volatility is significant (e.g., Q1 2024 net income of $3.19B vs. Q4 2023's $1.99B). The lack of cash flow data prevents assessment of cash conversion.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Debt-to-equity is 1.306. Cash of $15.20B covers 41.6% of total debt ($36.50B). Total equity of $27.95B provides a buffer, but net debt (debt - cash) is $21.30B.

**3. Valuation versus Own Supplied History**
- Current P/E diluted is 25.531, at the high end of its 1-year range of 21.6 to 27.7.
- EV/EBIT is 29.389, at the top of its 1-year range of 22.8 to 29.6.
- P/B is 9.844, near the top of its 1-year range of 8.3 to 10.2.
- Current valuation multiples are elevated relative to their own history, especially for P/E and EV/EBIT.

**4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context**
- Latest price (2024-06-28) is $63.65. The 52-week range is $51.55 to $64.36.
- Annual return (from 2023-06-28) is +5.2%.
- Quarterly return (from 2024-03-28) is +4.0%.
- The price has recovered from a low of $51.55, but the annual return of +5.2% is modest relative to the company's TTM net income growth of 2.3%.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
- **Upside:** Stable high gross margins (60%+) and a strengthened cash position ($15.20B) provide a defensive foundation. A reduction in debt-to-equity from 1.306 could improve financial flexibility.
- **Downside:** Elevated valuation (P/E 25.5, EV/EBIT 29.4) relative to minimal revenue growth (+2.9% YoY) and earnings growth (+2.8% YoY) presents multiple compression risk. The high debt load ($36.50B) remains a balance sheet burden.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The fundamental profile is characterized by stable, high margins but slow top- and bottom-line growth. Valuation is stretched versus its own history, pricing in modest growth at high multiples. The balance sheet is solvent but leveraged. Price returns have been positive but undifferentiated. The quantitative risk/reward appears unfavorable at this price level, as the current valuation does not offer a margin of safety relative to the company's recent, modest growth trajectory.
KO.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: Coca-Cola (KO)**
**Decision Date: 2024-07-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from 2023-02-21 10-K)**
Coca-Cola is a total beverage company with a global franchise operating in over 200 countries. Its business model is built on a vast portfolio of brands (e.g., Coca-Cola, Sprite, Fanta, Costa, fairlife) and a dual operating structure: high-margin concentrate operations and lower-margin finished product operations. The company's system relies on a network of independent bottling partners, with whom it has long-term agreements, and its own consolidated bottling operations. Key competitive strengths include unparalleled brand recognition, a sophisticated global distribution and marketing system, and a portfolio that spans sparkling soft drinks, water, sports drinks, coffee, tea, and juices. The business is seasonal, with higher sales in the second and third quarters.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated Evidence)**
*   **Dividend & Investor Focus (June 2024):** Multiple articles (2024-06-30, 2024-06-26) highlight KO as a "Dividend Aristocrat" and a core holding for income investors, though some note its high valuation and low yield relative to peers. One article (2024-06-29) suggests a competitor may be a more promising investment.
*   **Growth & Outlook (June 2024):** Web context indicates analysts see potential for a 10% surge, citing strong consumer demand and effective cost management (2024-06-05). Comparable operating margin improved to 32.4% in Q1 2024 (2024-06-09). Opportunities are identified in the health and wellness trend, specifically in low-sugar and zero-calorie products (2024-06-03, 2024-06-27).
*   **Legal & Regulatory Challenges (May-June 2024):** The company faces new litigation, including a lawsuit alleging deceptive marketing of sugar-sweetened drinks (2024-06-05) and a class action concerning PFAS in Simply Tropical juice (2024-06-20). A city has also sued for cleanup costs (2024-06-26). These actions are cited as regulatory and reputational risks.
*   **Competitive Landscape (June 2024):** A financial comparison (2024-06-25) suggests the cannabis market is set to outpace major CPG players like Coca-Cola in growth.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Legal & Regulatory:** Active lawsuits concerning product marketing and ingredients (PFAS) pose financial, operational, and reputational risks (2024-06-05, 2024-06-20, 2024-06-26).
    *   **Health & Consumer Trends:** Persistent consumer shift away from sugary drinks is a fundamental challenge, though the company is actively addressing it.
    *   **Competition:** Intense competition from global players (PepsiCo, Nestlé), regional brands, and emerging categories like cannabis (2024-06-25) and private-label products.
    *   **Input Costs & Sustainability:** Dependence on key raw materials and water, a limited resource, presents operational and sustainability risks (2023-02-21 10-K).
*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Brand & Distribution Moat:** Unmatched global brand portfolio and distribution network provide significant scale and pricing power (2023-02-21 10-K).
    *   **Portfolio Diversification & Innovation:** Active portfolio optimization toward growing categories (water, sports, coffee, low-sugar) mitigates reliance on traditional sodas (2024-06-03, 2024-06-27).
    *   **Financial Resilience:** Consistent dividend history and focus on cost management support investor confidence during market volatility (2024-06-30, 2024-06-05).

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral to Slightly Positive.** The evidence confirms Coca-Cola's durable franchise built on iconic brands and a global system, with recent narrative pointing to solid operational execution and margin improvement. However, the near-term picture is tempered by active legal challenges and a competitive landscape that includes both traditional rivals and new growth categories. The company's strategic focus on health trends and shareholder returns provides a positive offset, but the legal overhang and valuation concerns noted in the news warrant caution. The assessment is based on a strong foundational business facing identifiable, but manageable, headwinds.

corrections.issue: Quant report states Q1 2024 operating margin is 18.9% ($2.14B/$11.30B), but the raw data shows operating_income=2.14B and revenue=11.30B, which indeed yields ~18.9%. However, the qual report cites 'comparable operating margin reached 32.4% this quarter versus 31.8% in Q1 2023' from a Nasdaq article. The quant report also compares Q1 2024 operating margin (18.9%) to Q1 2023 (30.7%, calculated as $3.37B/$10.98B). The raw data confirms Q1 2023 operating_income=3.37B and revenue=10.98B, yielding ~30.7%. The discrepancy between 18.9% (quant) and 32.4% (qual/news) likely reflects 'comparable' vs GAAP operating income, but neither report flags this divergence. The quant report should note that the GAAP operating margin decline from 30.7% to 18.9% may reflect items that the 'comparable' metric strips out, rather than a true operational deterioration.
corrections.required_action: Add a note that the GAAP operating margin decline (30.7% to 18.9%) may be driven by non-comparable items; the qual report's cited 32.4% comparable margin suggests underlying operational improvement, not deterioration. This materially affects the downside narrative.

corrections.issue: The qual report states 'For the full year, revenue is expected to increase by 9.3% from $31.9 billion in 2018 to $34.8 billion in 2019, and further by 4.7% to $36.5 billion in 2020' from a Forbes article dated 2024-06-03. This is stale forward-looking data from 2018-2019 projections, not current guidance. TTM revenue as of Q1 2024 is $46.07B per the raw data. Citing 2018-era revenue forecasts as current context is misleading.
corrections.required_action: Remove or clearly caveat the Forbes revenue projection quote as historical/stale and not reflective of current fundamentals.

corrections.issue: The quant report's balance sheet section notes total_equity=27.95B and total_debt=36.50B, yielding debt-to-equity of 1.306. However, the raw balance sheet data for 2024-03-29 shows total_liabilities=-27.95B (negative sign), which appears to be a data artifact where total_liabilities equals total_equity in magnitude. The quant report does not flag this data anomaly. The debt-to-equity ratio of 1.306 is confirmed by the valuation ratios panel, so the calculation is consistent, but the raw balance sheet liability figure warrants a note about potential data quality issues.
corrections.required_action: Note the anomalous total_liabilities=-27.95B figure in the raw data for Q1 2024; the debt-to-equity ratio is confirmed from the valuation panel but the underlying balance sheet data quality should be flagged.

unsupported_claims: The qual report's Forbes citation (2024-06-03) states 'For the full year, revenue is expected to increase by 9.3% from $31.9 billion in 2018 to $34.8 billion in 2019' — this is stale 2018-era guidance presented alongside current analysis without caveat, and is not supported by any current data in the raw packs.

The quant report states Q1 2024 operating margin is 18.9% and characterizes it as 'lower than Q1 2023's 30.7%' implying operational deterioration, but the qual report's sourced article (2024-06-09) states comparable operating margin improved to 32.4% vs 31.8% — the quant report does not acknowledge this conflicting evidence from within the supplied packs.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Coca-Cola's durable franchise and stable margins are offset by stretched valuation (P/E 25.5 at top of range) and minimal growth (revenue +2.9% YoY, EPS +2.8% YoY), yielding a neutral risk-adjusted outlook. The stock offers modest dividend appeal but limited upside at current multiples.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Coca-Cola's durable franchise and stable margins are offset by stretched valuation (P/E 25.5 at top of range) and minimal growth (revenue +2.9% YoY, EPS +2.8% YoY), yielding a neutral risk-adjusted outlook. The stock offers modest dividend appeal but limited upside at current multiples.
key_factors: Valuation stretched at top of historical range: P/E of 25.5 (1-year range 21.6-27.7) and EV/EBIT of 29.4 (range 22.8-29.6) price in modest growth at premium multiples, leaving little margin of safety.
Minimal top- and bottom-line growth: TTM revenue of $46.07B with Q1 2024 YoY growth of only +2.9%, and EPS diluted of $0.74 up just +2.8% YoY, insufficient to justify elevated multiples.
Stable high gross margins (~60-63%) and strengthened cash position ($15.20B, covering 41.6% of $36.50B debt) provide defensive floor but do not drive re-rating.

Active legal overhangs including PFAS litigation (2024-06-20), deceptive marketing lawsuit (2024-06-05), and prior IRS dispute ($3B) create reputational and financial risk that the market has not fully priced.

Item 1.Business.
General
Originally organized as Caterpillar Tractor Co. in 1925 in the State of California, our company was reorganized as Caterpillar Inc. in 1986 in the State of Delaware.  As used herein, the term "Caterpillar," "we," "us," "our" or "the company" refers to Caterpillar Inc. and its subsidiaries unless designated or identified otherwise.
Overview
With 2022 sales and revenues of $59.427 billion, Caterpillar is the world's leading manufacturer of construction and mining equipment, off-highway diesel and natural gas engines, industrial gas turbines and diesel-electric locomotives.  The company principally operates through its three primary segments - Construction Industries, Resource Industries and Energy & Transportation - and also provides financing and related services through its Financial Products segment.  Caterpillar is also a leading U.S. exporter.  Through a global network of independent dealers and direct sales of certain products, Caterpillar builds long-term relationships with customers around the world.
Enterprise Strategy
Our company strategy, rolled out in 2017, reflects our legacy and our continuing commitment to meet the needs of our customers and the communities in which we live and work. United by our Values, Caterpillar employees around the world share a focused view of our business through the Operating & Execution Model, through which we are making strategic choices today to create long-term profitable growth. Since 2017, we focused on three strategic areas: Expanded Offerings, Operational Excellence and Services. In 2022, we updated our strategy to also include sustainability as a strategic focus area. For nearly 100 years, our longstanding commitment to sustainability has inspired us to set and achieve meaningful environmental, social and governance goals. It's also allowed us to develop innovative products, technologies and services to support our customers on their sustainability journey. The addition of sustainability as a focus area, together with operational excellence, expanded offerings and 
…[truncated 19945 chars]…
erpillar Insurance Co. Ltd., a wholly owned subsidiary of Caterpillar Insurance Holdings Inc., is a captive insurance company domiciled in Bermuda and regulated by the Bermuda Monetary Authority.  Caterpillar Insurance Co. Ltd. is registered as a Class 2 (General Business) and Class B (Long-Term) insurer with the Bermuda Monetary Authority.  Under its Class 2 insurance license, Caterpillar Insurance Co. Ltd. insures its parent and affiliates for general liability, property, auto liability and cargo.  It also provides reinsurance to Caterpillar Insurance Company under a quota share reinsurance agreement for its contractual liability and contractors' equipment programs in the United States. In addition, Caterpillar Insurance Co. Ltd. reinsures 100% of the international employee benefit plans of Caterpillar Inc. through retrocession agreements with other insurers.  The employee benefits coverages include medical and accident coverages. which are reported under its Class 2 insurance license and life and disability coverages, which are reported under its Class B insurance license.  The Bermuda Monetary Authority is responsible for monitoring compliance with solvency requirements and requires an Annual Financial Filing for this purpose.
Caterpillar Product Services Corporation (CPSC), a wholly owned subsidiary of Caterpillar, is a warranty company domiciled in Missouri.  CPSC previously conducted a machine extended service contract program in Germany and France by providing machine extended warranty reimbursement protection to dealers in Germany and France.  The program was discontinued effective January 1, 2013, though CPSC continues to provide extended warranty reimbursement protection under existing contracts.
Caterpillar Insurance Services Corporation, a wholly owned subsidiary of Caterpillar Insurance Holdings Inc., is a Tennessee insurance agency licensed in all 50 states, the District of Columbia and Guam. It provides brokerage and insurance services for all property and casualty and life and health lines of business.
Caterpillar's insurance group provides protection and service for claims under the following programs:
- Contractual Liability Insurance to insure certain service contract obligations of Caterpillar and its affiliates, Caterpillar dealers and original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
- Cargo reinsurance for the worldwide cargo risks of Caterpillar products.
5
Table of Contents
- Contractors' Equipment Physical Damage Insurance for equipment manufactured by Caterpillar or OEMs, which is leased, rented or sold by third party dealers to customers.
- General liability, employer's liability, auto liability and property insurance for Caterpillar.
- Life, disability, medical and accident reinsurance for Caterpillar's international employee benefits program (non-U.S.).
- Reinsurance to cover VEBA Trust for medical claims of certain Caterpillar retirees and dependents.
- Brokerage and insurance services for property and casualty and life and health business.
Competitive Environment
Caterpillar products and services are sold worldwide into a variety of highly competitive markets.  In all markets, we compete on the basis of product performance, customer service, quality and price.  From time to time, the intensity of competition results in price discounting in a particular industry or region.  Such price discounting puts pressure on margins and can negatively impact operating profit. Outside the United States, certain competitors enjoy competitive advantages inherent to operating in their home countries or regions.
Raw Materials and Component Products
We source our raw materials and manufactured components from suppliers both domestically and internationally. These purchases include unformed materials and rough and finished parts.  Unformed materials include a variety of steel products, which are then cut or formed to shape and machined in our facilities. Rough parts include various sized steel and iron castings and forgings, which are machined to final specification levels inside our facilities. Finished parts are ready to assemble components, which are made either to Caterpillar specifications or to supplier developed specifications.  We machine and assemble some of the components used in our machines, engines and power generation units and to support our after-market dealer parts sales. We also purchase various goods and services used in production, logistics, offices and product development processes.  We maintain global strategic sourcing models to meet our global facilities' production needs while building long-term supplier relationships and leveraging enterprise spend.  We expect our suppliers to maintain, at all times, industry-leading levels of quality and the ability to timely deliver raw materials and component products for our machine and engine products. However, in some cases, increases in demand or supply chain disruptions have led to parts and components constraints across some products. We use a variety of agreements with suppliers to protect our intellectual property and processes to monitor and mitigate risks of the supply base causing a business disruption.  The risks monitored include supplier financial viability, the ability to increase or decrease production levels, business continuity, quality and delivery.
Patents and Trademarks
We own a number of patents and trademarks, which have been obtained over a period of years and relate to the products we manufacture and the services we provide. These patents and trademarks are generally considered beneficial to our business. We do not regard our business as being dependent upon any single patent or group of patents.
Order Backlog
The dollar amount of backlog believed to be firm was approximately $30.4 billion at December 31, 2022 and $23.1 billion at December 31, 2021. Compared with year-end 2021, the order backlog increased for both the Energy & Transportation and Construction Industries segments, with the largest increase in Energy & Transportation. Of the total backlog at December 31, 2022, approximately $5.5 billion was not expected to be filled in 2023.
Dealers and Distributors
We distribute our machines principally through a worldwide organization of dealers (dealer network), 43 located in the United States and 113 located outside the United States, serving 192 countries.  We sell reciprocating engines principally through the dealer network and to other manufacturers for use in products. We also sell some of the reciprocating engines manufactured by our subsidiary Perkins Engines Company Limited through its worldwide network of 88 distributors covering 185 countries.  We sell the FG Wilson branded electric power generation systems through its worldwide network of 110 distributors covering 109 countries.  We also sell some of the large, medium speed reciprocating engines under the MaK brand through a worldwide network of 20 distributors covering 130 countries.
6
Table of Contents
Our dealers do not deal exclusively with our products; however, in most cases sales and servicing of our products are the dealers' principal business.  We sell some products, primarily turbines and locomotives, directly to end customers through sales forces employed by the company.  At times, these employees are assisted by independent sales representatives.
While the large majority of our worldwide dealers are independently owned and operated, we own and operate a dealership in Japan that covers approximately 80% of the Japanese market:  Nippon Caterpillar Division.  We are currently operating this Japanese dealer directly and we report its results in the All Other operating segment.  There are also three independent dealers in the Southern Region of Japan.

CAT.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: CAT (Caterpillar Inc.)**  
*Decision Date: 2024-07-01 | Evidence Point-in-Time: ≤ 2024-06-28*

### 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory
- **Revenue**: Latest Q1 2024 revenue was $15.80B, down 7.4% QoQ but essentially flat YoY (-0.4%). TTM revenue is $67.00B.
- **Earnings**: Q1 2024 EPS diluted was $5.75, up 9.1% QoQ and 53.7% YoY. Net income was $2.85B, up 6.8% QoQ and 47.0% YoY. TTM net income is $11.24B.
- **Margins**: Q1 2024 gross margin was 38.9% ($6.14B/$15.80B), operating margin 22.3% ($3.52B/$15.80B), net margin 18.0% ($2.85B/$15.80B). All margins have expanded YoY.
- **Cash-Flow**: No quarterly or TTM cash-flow data provided.
- **Balance Sheet**: Total assets $83.74B, total liabilities $66.10B, total equity $17.64B. Equity decreased QoQ from $19.50B (2023-12-31) but increased from $15.76B (2022-06-30). Debt-to-equity ratio is 3.75 ($66.10B/$17.64B).

### 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience
- **Earnings Quality**: Strong YoY EPS growth (+53.7%) on flat revenue (-0.4%) indicates significant margin expansion. However, the absence of cash-flow data prevents assessment of cash conversion.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: High leverage (debt-to-equity 3.75) and a QoQ decline in equity ($19.50B to $17.64B) indicate elevated financial risk. Total liabilities have consistently exceeded $65B over the past two years.

### 3. Valuation Versus Supplied History
- **Current Valuation (2024-06-28)**: PE diluted = 14.721, P/B = 9.38, P/S = 2.471, earnings yield = 6.8%.
- **Historical Range (2023-07-03 to 2024-06-28)**: PE diluted ranged from 13.0 to 21.1; P/B ranged from 5.8 to 10.2. Current PE is near the low end of its range, while P/B is near the high end.
- **Margin/ROE Context**: Current net margin (0.168) and ROE (0.637) are at the high end of their respective historical ranges (net margin 0.1–0.2, ROE 0.4–0.6).

### 4. Price Level and Return Context Versus Fundamentals
- **Price Level**: Latest close $333.10 (2024-06-28), down from a 52-week high of $382.01.
- **Returns**: Quarterly return (from 2024-03-28) is -9.1%; annual return (from 2023-06-28) is +37.7%.
- **Fundamental Alignment**: The annual price gain (+37.7%) aligns with strong YoY earnings growth (+53.7% EPS) and margin expansion. The recent quarterly price decline (-9.1%) coincides with a QoQ revenue drop (-7.4%) and flat YoY revenue.

### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside
- **Upside**: Strong earnings growth momentum (EPS +53.7% YoY) and improving margins. Valuation (PE 14.7) is low relative to its recent history (range 13.0–21.1).
- **Downside**: High financial leverage (debt-to-equity 3.75) and flat revenue growth (-0.4% YoY). Absence of cash-flow data raises uncertainty about earnings quality. Equity declined QoQ, and P/B is elevated at 9.38.

### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion
The stock exhibits strong earnings growth and margin expansion, with a PE ratio near the low end of its historical range. However, balance-sheet risk is significant due to high leverage, and revenue growth has stalled. The recent price pullback (-9.1% quarterly) may reflect these fundamental concerns. Without cash-flow data, earnings durability is uncertain. Risk/reward appears balanced but leans cautious due to leverage and lack of cash-flow visibility.
CAT.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: CAT (Caterpillar Inc.)**
**Decision Date: 2024-07-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from 10-K, 2023-02-15)**
Caterpillar is the world's leading manufacturer of construction and mining equipment, off-highway engines, industrial gas turbines, and diesel-electric locomotives. It operates through four primary segments: Construction Industries, Resource Industries, Energy & Transportation, and Financial Products. The business is supported by a vast global network of independent dealers and a strategy focused on operational excellence, expanded offerings, services, and sustainability. The company holds a significant order backlog, which was $30.4 billion as of December 31, 2022.

**2. Recent Developments (PIT: 2024-05-12 to 2024-06-29)**
*   **Earnings Growth & Outlook (2024-05-30, 2024-06-26):** Narrative indicates the company has benefited from improving end-market demand and cost control, leading to a 13-quarter stint of earnings growth. An analyst upgrade (Citi, 2024-06-26) cited expectations for positive earnings growth, higher margins, and less volatility.
*   **Capital Allocation - Dividends (2024-06-21):** News noted the company recently announced a dividend hike.
*   **Stock Performance & Sentiment (2024-06-18, 2024-06-24, 2024-06-26):** The stock was reported as outperforming the market and was highlighted as a top "value" stock by one service. Analyst sentiment was described as mixed, with opinions spanning bullish to bearish.
*   **ESG/Reputational Risk (2024-06-24, 2024-06-29):** A shareholder proposal was submitted regarding the consistency of Caterpillar's lobbying with its stated goals. Separately, a Norwegian pension fund divested a $69 million stake over alleged human rights concerns related to the company's products' use in a conflict zone (Gaza).

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Geopolitical & ESG Scrutiny (2024-06-24, 2024-06-29):** The company faces reputational and potential investor flight risks from controversies regarding the use of its equipment in conflict zones, leading to divestments by entities citing human rights violations.
    *   *Offset:* The company's broad-based strategy includes sustainability as a key focus area, which may help it manage and mitigate such risks over the long term (10-K).
*   **Risk: Cyclical End-Market Demand:** Business performance is inherently tied to global construction, mining, and energy infrastructure activity, making it sensitive to economic cycles.
    *   *Offset:* Recent narrative (2024-05-30, 2024-06-26) points to improving demand and a large order backlog ($30.4B as of 2022-12-31) providing near-term visibility.
*   **Risk: Supply Chain & Operational Execution:** The 10-K notes that demand increases or supply chain disruptions can lead to component constraints.
    *   *Offset:* The company cites robust product development, manufacturing processes, and global strategic sourcing models to mitigate these risks.

**4. Qualitative Assessment: Neutral**
The assessment is **Neutral**, balancing clear near-term operational positives against significant external risks.
*   **Positive:** The company's franchise is solid as the global market leader. Recent narrative points to sustained earnings growth, positive analyst upgrades, and shareholder returns via dividend hikes.
*   **Negative:** A material and recent negative development is the divestment by a pension fund and shareholder activism related to geopolitical ESG risks. This introduces reputational and potential financial volatility that counterbalances the operational momentum.

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'Current net margin (0.168) and ROE (0.637) are at the high end of their respective historical ranges (net margin 0.1–0.2, ROE 0.4–0.6).' However, the raw valuation data shows ROE values in the sparse history reaching 0.637 at the latest point (2024-05-24 and 2024-06-28), which is above the stated range ceiling of 0.6. The report correctly notes this is 'at the high end' but the range should be stated as 0.4–0.637 to be precise, or the report should note ROE has broken above its prior range.
corrections.required_action: Minor precision issue. No action required for decision-making, but the ROE range should be noted as having been breached at the top end.

corrections.issue: The qual report references 'a 13-quarter stint of earnings growth' sourced from a 2024-05-30 article. The raw quantitative data only provides 8 quarters of history (Q2 2022 through Q1 2024). Within the available data, EPS did decline QoQ in Q4 2023 (5.27 vs Q3 5.45) and Q2 2023 (5.67 vs Q1 2023's 3.74 was an increase, but Q3 2023 at 5.45 was below Q2's 5.67). The '13-quarter streak' claim cannot be fully verified from the supplied data and may refer to YoY growth rather than sequential QoQ growth. This is a narrative claim from external news, not a financial figure invented by the specialist, so it is acceptable as cited but should not be treated as verified.
corrections.required_action: No action required. The claim is properly attributed to an external source. PM should note this is analyst narrative, not independently verified from the supplied financial data.

corrections.issue: The qual report states the order backlog was '$30.4 billion as of December 31, 2022' and uses this as an offset to cyclical risk. The raw 10-K text confirms this figure. However, this backlog data is 18 months old as of the decision date (2024-07-01) and may not reflect current conditions. The report does not flag this staleness.
corrections.required_action: Minor. The backlog figure is confirmed in the 10-K but is dated 2022-12-31. PM should note this is not current backlog data and more recent figures may exist in subsequent filings not supplied.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: CAT's strong earnings growth (+53.7% YoY EPS) and margin expansion are compelling, but the stock's valuation is no longer cheap (P/B 9.38 near historical highs), revenue growth has stalled (-0.4% YoY), and balance sheet leverage remains elevated (D/E 3.75). The recent -9.1% quarterly pullback offers a modest entry point, but without cash flow visibility and with emerging ESG/reputational risks, the risk/reward is balanced rather than clearly attractive.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: CAT's strong earnings growth (+53.7% YoY EPS) and margin expansion are compelling, but the stock's valuation is no longer cheap (P/B 9.38 near historical highs), revenue growth has stalled (-0.4% YoY), and balance sheet leverage remains elevated (D/E 3.75). The recent -9.1% quarterly pullback offers a modest entry point, but without cash flow visibility and with emerging ESG/reputational risks, the risk/reward is balanced rather than clearly attractive.
key_factors: Q1 2024 EPS of $5.75 grew +53.7% YoY on flat revenue (-0.4% YoY), demonstrating significant margin expansion with net margin reaching 18.0% and ROE at 0.637 — both at or above historical highs.

P/E of 14.7 sits near the low end of its 13.0–21.1 historical range, but P/B of 9.38 is near the top of its 5.8–10.2 range, reflecting that the market has already priced in much of the earnings improvement.

Revenue growth has stalled at -0.4% YoY (Q1 2024 $15.80B) with a QoQ decline of -7.4%, and the $30.4B backlog cited as visibility is 18 months old (as of 2022-12-31), raising questions about forward demand.

High financial leverage (debt-to-equity 3.75) and absent cash flow data create balance sheet risk, while recent ESG controversies — including a Norwegian pension fund divestment over human rights concerns — introduce reputational and investor flight risk.

ITEM 1. BUSINESS
General
Walmart Inc. ("Walmart," the "Company" or "we") is a people-led, technology-powered omni-channel retailer dedicated to help people around the world save money and live better – anytime and anywhere – by providing the opportunity to shop in both retail stores and through eCommerce, and to access our other service offerings.  Through innovation, we strive to continuously improve a customer-centric experience that seamlessly integrates our eCommerce and retail stores in an omni-channel offering that saves time for our customers.  Each week, we serve approximately 240 million customers who visit more than 10,500 stores and numerous eCommerce websites in 20 countries.
Our strategy is to make every day easier for busy families, operate with discipline, sharpen our culture and become more digital, and make trust a competitive advantage.  Making life easier for busy families includes our commitment to price leadership, which has been and will remain a cornerstone of our business, as well as increasing convenience to save our customers time.  By leading on price, we earn the trust of our customers every day by providing a broad assortment of quality merchandise and services at everyday low prices ("EDLP").  EDLP is our pricing philosophy under which we price items at a low price every day so our customers trust that our prices will not change under frequent promotional activity.  Everyday low cost ("EDLC") is our commitment to control expenses so our cost savings can be passed along to our customers.
Our operations comprise three reportable segments: Walmart U.S., Walmart International and Sam's Club.  Our fiscal year ends on January 31 for our United States ("U.S.") and Canadian operations.  We consolidate all other operations generally using a one-month lag and on a calendar year basis.  Our discussion is as of and for the fiscal years ended January 31, 2023 ("fiscal 2023"), January 31, 2022 ("fiscal 2022") and January 31, 2
…[truncated 20041 chars]…
 and to deliver a customer-centric omni-channel experience largely determines our competitive position within the retail industry.  We believe price leadership is a critical part of our business model and we continue to focus on moving our markets towards an EDLP approach.  Additionally, our ability to operate food departments effectively has a significant impact on our competitive position in the markets where we operate.  Each of these landscapes is highly competitive and rapidly evolving, and new business models and the entry of new, well-funded competitors continue to intensify this competition.  Some of our competitors have longer histories in these lines of business, more customers, and greater brand recognition.  They may be able to obtain more favorable terms from suppliers and business partners and to devote greater resources to the development of these businesses.  In addition, for eCommerce and other internet-based businesses, newer or smaller businesses may be better able to innovate and compete with us.
Distribution.
We utilize a total of 188 distribution facilities located in Canada, Central America, Chile, China, India, Mexico and South Africa.  Through these facilities, we process and distribute both imported and domestic products to the operating units of the Walmart International segment.  During fiscal 2023, the majority of Walmart International's purchases passed through these distribution facilities.  Suppliers ship the remainder of Walmart International's purchases directly to our stores in the various markets in which we operate.  Across the segment, we have efficient networks connecting physical stores and distribution and fulfillment centers which facilitate the movement of goods to where our customers live.  We ship merchandise purchased by customers on our eCommerce platforms by a number of methods from multiple locations including from our 100 dedicated eCommerce fulfillment centers, more than 3,600 eCommerce sort centers and last-mile delivery facilities in India, as well as our physical retail stores.
Sam's Club Segment
Sam's Club operates in 44 states in the U.S. and in Puerto Rico.  Sam's Club is a membership-only warehouse club that also operates samsclub.com.  Sam's Club had net sales of $84.3 billion for fiscal 2023, representing 14% of our consolidated fiscal 2023 net sales, and had net sales of $73.6 billion and $63.9 billion for fiscal 2022 and 2021, respectively.  As a membership-only warehouse club, membership income is a significant component of the segment's operating income.  Sam's Club operates with a lower gross profit rate and lower operating expenses as a percentage of net sales than our other segments.
Membership.
The following two options are available to members:
|  | Plus Membership | Club Membership |
| Annual Membership Fee | $110 | $50 |
| Number of Add-on Memberships ($45 each) | Up to 16 | Up to 8 |
All memberships include a spouse/household card at no additional cost.  Plus Members are also eligible for free shipping on the majority of merchandise, with no minimum order size, and receive discounts on prescriptions and glasses.  Beginning in fiscal 2023, Sam's Club launched a single loyalty rewards currency called Sam's Cash which merges and replaces existing Cash Rewards for Plus members and Cash Back for Sam's Club Mastercard holders.  Members may redeem Sam's Cash on purchases in the club and online, to pay for membership fees or for cash in clubs.  Sam's Cash does not expire and is available for monthly redemption.
Omni-channel.
Sam's Club provides an omni-channel experience to members, integrating warehouse clubs and eCommerce through such services as Curbside Pickup, mobile Scan & Go, ship-from-club, and delivery-from-club.  Members have access to a broad assortment of merchandise and services, including those not found in our clubs, online at samsclub.com and through our mobile commerce applications.  The warehouse facility sizes generally range between 32,000 and 168,000 square feet, with an average size of approximately 134,000 square feet.
Merchandise.
Sam's Club offers merchandise in the following five merchandise categories:
- Grocery and consumables includes dairy, meat, bakery, deli, produce, dry, chilled or frozen packaged foods, alcoholic and nonalcoholic beverages, floral, snack foods, candy, other grocery items, health and beauty aids, paper goods, laundry and home care, baby care, pet supplies and other consumable items;
- Fuel, tobacco and other categories;
10
- Home and apparel includes home improvement, outdoor living, gardening, furniture, apparel, jewelry, tools and power equipment, housewares, toys, seasonal items, mattresses, and tire and battery centers;
- Health and wellness includes pharmacy, optical and hearing services and over-the-counter drugs; and
- Technology, office and entertainment includes consumer electronics and accessories, software, video games, office supplies, appliances, and third-party gift cards.
Within the categories above, the Member's Mark private label brand continues to expand its assortment and deliver member value.
Operations.
Sam's Club is available to members through warehouse club locations, as well as online or through the mobile application 24 hours a day.  Club locations offer Plus Members the ability to shop before regular operating hours.  Consistent with its strategy, Sam's Club continues to develop technology tools to drive a great member experience.  Curbside Pickup is available at all clubs to help provide fast, easy and contact-free shopping for members.  Sam's Club also offers "Scan & Go," a mobile checkout and payment solution, which allows members to bypass the checkout line.
Seasonal Aspects of Operations.
Sam's Club's business is seasonal to a certain extent due to calendar events and national and religious holidays, as well as different weather patterns.  Historically, its highest sales volume has occurred in the fiscal quarter ending January 31.
Competition.
Sam's Club competes with other membership-only warehouse clubs, the largest of which is Costco, as well as with discount retailers, retail and wholesale grocers, general merchandise wholesalers and distributors, gasoline stations as well as omni-channel and eCommerce retailers and catalog businesses.  At Sam's Club, we provide value at members-only prices, a quality merchandise assortment, and bulk sizing to serve both our Plus and Club members.  Our eCommerce website and mobile commerce applications have increasingly become important factors in our ability to compete.
Distribution.
We utilize 29 dedicated distribution facilities located strategically throughout the U.S., as well as some of the Walmart U.S. segment's distribution facilities which service the Sam's Club segment for certain items.  During fiscal 2023, the majority of Sam's Club's non-fuel club purchases were shipped from these facilities, while the remainder of our purchases were shipped directly to Sam's Club locations by suppliers.  Sam's Club ships merchandise purchased on samsclub.com and through its mobile commerce applications by a number of methods including shipments made directly from clubs, 13 dedicated eCommerce fulfillment centers and other distribution centers.
Sam's Club uses a combination of our private truck fleet, as well as common carriers, to transport perishable and non-perishable merchandise from distribution facilities to clubs.
Intellectual Property
We regard our trademarks, service marks, copyrights, patents, domain names, trade dress, trade secrets, proprietary technologies, and similar intellectual property as important to our success, and with respect to our associates, customers and others, we rely on trademark, copyright, and patent law, trade-secret protection, and confidentiality and/or license agreements to protect our proprietary rights. **Quantitative Report: WMT (as of 2024-07-01)**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
*   **Revenue & Earnings:** Latest quarter (2024-04-30) revenue was 161.51B, up 6.0% YoY from 152.30B. Quarterly revenue growth is volatile, ranging from 160.80B (Q3 2023) to 173.39B (Q4 2024). Latest diluted EPS was 0.63, up 1.6% YoY from 0.62. The trailing four-quarter (TTM) net income is 19.68B on revenue of 657.33B.
*   **Margins:** Gross margin for Q1 2024 was 24.8% (gross profit 40.08B / revenue 161.51B). Operating margin was 4.2% (operating income 6.84B / revenue 161.51B). Net margin for the period was 3.3%.
*   **Cash Flow:** Cash flow statement data is unavailable for the reported periods.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total equity increased from 79.23B (2023-04-30) to 88.07B (2024-04-30). Total assets grew from 245.05B to 254.05B. Total debt as of 2024-01-31 was 39.58B against total equity of 90.35B.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** Quarterly net income is volatile, featuring losses in Q4 2022 (EPS -0.66) and Q4 2024 (EPS -1.8). The TTM net margin is 3.0% (19.68B / 657.33B). The wide EPS range (from -1.8 to 2.92 in the last 8 quarters) indicates significant non-operating or non-recurring items.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The debt-to-equity ratio at the last available date (2024-01-31) was 0.44 (39.58B / 90.35B). Total equity has consistently been positive, with a low of 80.12B (2022-10-31). Cash flow data is required to assess full resilience.

**3. Valuation vs. Supplied History**
*   As of 2024-06-28, the P/E (diluted) was 27.8 and the P/B was 6.2. Over the prior year, the P/E range was 8.3 to 35.2, and the P/B range was 1.6 to 6.3.
*   The current valuation multiples are at the high end of their historical ranges. Net margin (0.03) and ROE (0.223) have improved from year-ago levels of 0.018 and 0.14, respectively.

**4. Price Level & Return Context**
*   The stock price on 2024-06-28 was 67.71. The 52-week high was 69.04 and the low was 49.85.
*   The quarterly return (approx. 63 trading days from 2024-03-28) was +12.5%. The annual return (approx. 252 trading days from 2023-06-28) was +30.8%. The price has significantly outperformed the recent fundamental improvement (e.g., Q1 YoY revenue +6.0%, EPS +1.6%).

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** The ROE has expanded to 22.3% from 14.0% one year prior. If sustained, this could support the current elevated P/B multiple.
*   **Downside:** Valuation is stretched relative to history (P/B near peak). The strong price advance (+30.8% annually) may have priced in recent fundamental improvements. Earnings volatility, with recent quarterly losses, introduces unpredictability.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The stock trades at a historical premium on both P/E and P/B bases following a +30.8% annual price advance. While returns on equity have improved, the valuation appears to reflect these gains. The lack of cash flow data and persistent quarterly earnings volatility add uncertainty. Based on the point-in-time evidence, the risk/reward profile is currently skewed toward the downside, as the price has run ahead of the measured fundamental trajectory.
WMT.qualitative_report:
### **Qualitative Research Report: Walmart Inc. (WMT)**
**Decision Date: 2024-07-01 | Point-in-Time Evidence: Through 2024-06-30**

#### **1. Business/Franchise Read (from 10-K filed 2023-03-17)**
Walmart Inc. is a global, people-led, technology-powered omni-channel retailer. Its core strategy is to be a price leader ("Everyday Low Price") for busy families, leveraging a seamlessly integrated physical and digital (omni-channel) experience to save customers time and money. The franchise is built on scale, operational discipline, and a "flywheel" of mutually reinforcing offerings.

*   **Structure:** Three reportable segments—Walmart U.S. (69% of net sales), Walmart International (17%), and Sam's Club (14%). Walmart U.S. operates hypermarkets (Supercenters), discount stores, and Neighborhood Markets. Sam's Club is a membership-based warehouse club. The International segment operates through wholly- or majority-owned subsidiaries in 19 countries.
*   **Key Strategic Pillars (as of FY2023):**
    *   **Price Leadership & Trust (EDLP/EDLC):** The foundational pricing philosophy.
    *   **Omni-Channel Integration:** A vast network of stores serving as fulfillment hubs for eCommerce, with services like pickup, delivery, and the Walmart+ membership program.
    *   **Expanding "Flywheel":** Growth beyond retail into higher-margin services, including Walmart Connect (advertising), Walmart Fulfillment Services, healthcare (Walmart Health), and financial services (including the PhonePe digital payments platform in India and the ONE fintech venture).
*   **Scale:** Serves ~240 million customers weekly across over 10,500 stores and eCommerce sites in 20 countries.

#### **2. Recent Narrative Developments (Dated, from Supplied Text)**
*   **2024-05-15 / 2024-05-16:** Walmart reported Q1 FY2025 results that beat expectations and **raised its full-year guidance**. It projected net sales growth to reach the high-end or slightly above the original 3-4% range and operating income growth at the high-end or slightly above the original 4-6% range. The stock surged to a record high, with reports attributing strength to easing inflation driving sales in groceries and non-essential merchandise.
*   **2024-06-27 / 2024-06-30:** Subsequent news focused on comparative stock performance versus peers like Costco and Amazon, and inclusion in "Dividend Aristocrat" or quality value ETF discussions. These items contained no new operational or financial guidance.

#### **3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets (from Supplied Text)**
*   **Legal & Regulatory Risk:**
    *   **Confirmed:** On **2024-06-18**, Walmart agreed to a **$1.64 million settlement** with the New Jersey Attorney General to resolve allegations of unconscionable business practices and pricing violations. The settlement includes a compliance order.
    *   **Narrative/Accusational:** A **2024-05-21** shareholder submission (Notice of Exempt Solicitation) cited risks from failing to address racial discrimination. A **2024-05-30** article alleged historical predatory pricing in 203 markets. These are presented as claims or allegations within the evidence, not confirmed legal outcomes.
    *   **Offset:** The New Jersey settlement amount appears modest relative to Walmart's scale. The 10-K describes a competitive, highly regulated environment and outlines legal compliance programs.
*   **Intense Competition:** The 10-K extensively details a highly competitive and rapidly evolving landscape across all segments (e.g., against brick-and-mortar, eCommerce, omni-channel, and service-based competitors). This is a persistent, core business risk.

#### **4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Cautiously Positive.**

The franchise is fundamentally robust, with a dominant scale, a clear strategy centered on price and omni-channel integration, and a demonstrated ability to expand its service ecosystem. The most recent material development (**2024-05-16**) was strongly positive: better-than-expected Q1 results and a raised full-year outlook, signaling operational momentum. However, the business exists in a fiercely competitive environment and faces ongoing legal and regulatory scrutiny, as evidenced by a dated settlement (**2024-06-18**) and other narrative allegations. The recent positive guidance update provides a meaningful counterweight to these persistent risks.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Cash flow statement data is unavailable for the reported periods' and later says 'Cash flow data is required to assess full resilience.' This is accurate per the raw packs (all cash flow entries show 'no data'). However, the report does not flag this as a material gap in its final conclusion with sufficient weight — the TTM operating income of 27.61B is available and could partially proxy for cash generation capacity, but the report doesn't mention it in the cash flow context.
corrections.required_action: No correction needed; the report correctly notes the absence. Minor note: TTM operating income (27.61B) is available in the packs and could be cited as a partial proxy for earnings power, but this is a stylistic suggestion, not an error.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'The trailing four-quarter (TTM) net income is 19.68B on revenue of 657.33B' and separately 'TTM net margin is 3.0% (19.68B / 657.33B).' The raw pack confirms TTM net_income=19.68B and TTM revenue=657.33B, so 19.68/657.33 = 2.99% ≈ 3.0%. This is consistent. However, the report also states 'Net margin for the period was 3.3%' for Q1 2024. Checking: 5.31B / 161.51B = 3.29% ≈ 3.3%. This is also consistent. No error here — verified.
corrections.required_action: No action needed; numerics verified.

corrections.issue: The qual report's section on the 10-K states 'Walmart U.S. had net sales of $420.6 billion for fiscal 2023, representing 69% of our fiscal 2023 consolidated net sales.' The raw 10-K text confirms this. The qual report's summary of segment structure (69% US, 17% International, 14% Sam's Club) is accurate per the filing. No issue.
corrections.required_action: No action needed.

unsupported_claims: Quant report: 'The wide EPS range (from -1.8 to 2.92 in the last 8 quarters) indicates significant non-operating or non-recurring items.' The raw packs show the EPS range is indeed -1.8 to 2.92, but the packs do not provide any breakdown of operating vs. non-operating items or identify any specific non-recurring charges. The attribution to 'non-operating or non-recurring items' is an inference not supported by the supplied evidence — the packs only show aggregate EPS figures.

Quant report: 'The stock price has significantly outperformed the recent fundamental improvement (e.g., Q1 YoY revenue +6.0%, EPS +1.6%).' While the price return (+30.8% annual) and fundamental growth figures are accurate per the packs, the characterization of 'significantly outperformed' is a qualitative judgment. The packs also show TTM net income growth of +179.9% YoY and ROE expansion from 14% to 22.3%, which are substantial fundamental improvements not mentioned in this comparison. This selective framing could mislead.
