pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Walmart's franchise is strong and recent guidance raises are encouraging, but the stock has already re-rated to historical valuation extremes (P/E 27.8, P/B 6.2 near peak) after a +30.8% annual advance, leaving limited upside for new entrants over the next year.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Walmart's franchise is strong and recent guidance raises are encouraging, but the stock has already re-rated to historical valuation extremes (P/E 27.8, P/B 6.2 near peak) after a +30.8% annual advance, leaving limited upside for new entrants over the next year.
key_factors: Valuation stretched: P/E of 27.8 and P/B of 6.2 are near the top of their one-year ranges (P/E 8.3–35.2, P/B 1.6–6.3), pricing in much of the recent fundamental improvement.

Operational momentum positive: Q1 FY2025 results beat expectations and management raised full-year guidance (net sales growth at high-end of 3-4%, operating income growth at high-end of 4-6%) as of 2024-05-16.

Earnings volatility persists: Quarterly EPS has ranged from -1.8 to 2.92 over the last eight quarters, and the latest Q1 EPS of 0.63 grew only 1.6% YoY despite strong revenue growth of 6.0%, indicating margin pressure.
ROE expansion supportive but priced in: ROE improved to 22.3% from 14.0% a year ago, yet the stock's +30.8% annual return has already captured this re-rating.

Item 1. Business
The Boeing Company, together with its subsidiaries (herein referred to as "Boeing," the "Company," "we," "us," "our"), is one of the world's major aerospace firms.
We are organized based on the products and services we offer. We operate in four reportable segments:
- 
Commercial Airplanes (BCA);
- 
Defense, Space & Security (BDS);
- 
Global Services (BGS);
- 
Boeing Capital (BCC).
Commercial Airplanes Segment
This segment develops, produces and markets commercial jet aircraft principally to the commercial airline industry worldwide. We are a leading producer of commercial aircraft and offer a family of commercial jetliners designed to meet a broad spectrum of global passenger and cargo requirements of airlines. This family of commercial jet aircraft in production includes the 737 narrow-body model and the 767, 777 and 787 wide-body models. We ended production of the 747 wide-body model in 2022. Development continues on the 777X program and the 737-7 and 737-10 derivatives.
Defense, Space & Security Segment
This segment engages in the research, development, production and modification of manned and unmanned military aircraft and weapons systems for strike, surveillance and mobility, including fighter and trainer aircraft; vertical lift, including rotorcraft and tilt-rotor aircraft; and commercial derivative aircraft, including anti-submarine and tanker aircraft. In addition, this segment engages in the research, development, production and modification of the following products and related services: strategic defense and intelligence systems, including strategic missile and defense systems, command, control, communications, computers, intelligence, surveillance and reconnaissance (C4ISR),
…[truncated 8603 chars]…
ion (FAA) and the Department of Homeland Security. Similar government authorities exist in our non-U.S. markets.
Government Contracts.
The U.S. government, and other governments, may terminate any of our government contracts at their convenience, as well as for default based on our failure to meet specified performance requirements. If any of our U.S. government contracts were to be terminated for convenience, we generally would be entitled to receive payment for work completed and allowable termination or cancellation costs. If any of our government contracts were to be terminated for default, generally the U.S. government would pay only for the work that has been accepted and could require us to pay the difference between the original contract price and the cost to re-procure the contract items, net of the work accepted from the original contract. The U.S. government can also hold us liable for damages resulting from the default.
Commercial Aircraft.
In the U.S., our commercial aircraft products are required to comply with FAA regulations governing production and quality systems, airworthiness and installation approvals, repair procedures and continuing operational safety. New aircraft models and new derivative aircraft are required to obtain FAA certification prior to entry into service. Outside the U.S., similar requirements exist for airworthiness, installation and operational approvals. These requirements are generally administered by the national aviation authorities of each country and, in the case of Europe, coordinated by the European Union Aviation Safety Agency.
Environmental.
We are subject to various federal, state, local and non-U.S. laws and regulations relating to environmental protection, including the discharge, treatment, storage, disposal and remediation of hazardous substances and wastes. We could also be affected by laws and regulations relating to climate change, including laws limiting or otherwise related to greenhouse gas emissions. These laws and regulations could lead to increased environmental compliance expenditures, increased energy and raw materials costs and new and/or additional investment in designs and technologies. We continually assess our compliance status and management of environmental matters to ensure our operations are in compliance with all applicable environmental laws and regulations. Investigation, remediation and operation and maintenance costs associated with environmental compliance and management of sites are a normal, recurring part of our operations. These costs often are allowable costs under our contracts with the U.S. government. It is reasonably possible that costs incurred to ensure continued environmental compliance could have a material impact on our results of operations, financial condition or cash flows if additional work requirements or more stringent clean-up standards are imposed by regulators, new areas of soil, air and groundwater contamination are discovered and/or expansions of work scope are prompted by the results of investigations.
For additional information relating to environmental contingencies, see Note 13 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.
Non-U.S. Sales.
Our non-U.S. sales are subject to both U.S. and non-U.S. governmental regulations and procurement policies and practices, including regulations relating to import-export control, tariffs, investment, exchange controls, anti-corruption and repatriation of earnings. Non-U.S. sales are also subject to varying currency, political and economic risks.
Raw Materials, Parts and Subassemblies
We are highly dependent on the availability of essential materials, parts and subassemblies from our suppliers and subcontractors. The most important raw materials required for our aerospace products
4
Table of Contents
are aluminum (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions), titanium (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions) and composites (including carbon and boron). Although alternative sources generally exist for these raw materials, qualification of the sources could take a year or more. During 2022, as a result of the Russia Ukraine war, we suspended purchasing titanium from Russia. This has not disrupted our operations as we have been able to use inventory on hand and identify alternative sources. Many major components and product equipment items are procured or subcontracted on a sole-source basis. We continue to work with a small number of sole-source suppliers to ensure continuity of supply for certain items.
Suppliers
We are dependent upon the ability of a large number of U.S. and non-U.S. suppliers and subcontractors to meet performance specifications, quality standards and delivery schedules at our anticipated costs. While we maintain an extensive qualification and performance surveillance system to control risk associated with such reliance on third parties, failure of suppliers or subcontractors to meet commitments could adversely affect production schedules and program/contract profitability, thereby jeopardizing our ability to fulfill commitments to our customers. We are also dependent on the availability of energy sources, such as electricity, at affordable prices.
Seasonality
No material portion of our business is considered to be seasonal.
Executive Officers of the Registrant
See "Item 10. Directors, Executive Officers and Corporate Governance" in Part III.
Other Information
Boeing was originally incorporated in the State of Washington in 1916 and reincorporated in Delaware in 1934. Our principal executive offices are located at 929 Long Bridge Drive, Arlington, Virginia 22202, and our telephone number is (703) 465-3500.
General information about us can be found at www.boeing.com. The information contained on or connected to our website is not incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K and should not be considered part of this or any other report filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Our Annual Report on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q and Current Reports on Form 8-K, as well as any amendments to those reports, are available free of charge through our website as soon as reasonably practicable after we file them with, or furnish them to, the SEC. The SEC maintains a website at www.sec.gov that contains reports, proxy statements and other information regarding SEC registrants, including Boeing.
Forward-Looking Statements
This report, as well as our annual report to shareholders, quarterly reports and other filings we make with the SEC, press and earnings releases and other written and oral communications, contain "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Words such as "may," "should," "expects," "intends," "projects," "plans," "believes," "estimates," "targets," "anticipates" and similar expressions generally identify these forward-looking statements. Examples of forward-looking statements include statements relating to our future financial condition and operating results, as well as any other statement that does not directly relate to any historical or current fact.

Forward-looking statements are based on expectations and assumptions that we believe to be reasonable when made, but that may not prove to be accurate. These statements are not guarantees and are subject to risks, uncertainties and changes in circumstances that are difficult to predict. Many factors, including those set forth in the "Risk Factors" section below and other important factors disclosed in this report and from time to time in our other filings with the SEC, could cause actual results to differ materially and adversely from these forward-looking statements. Any forward-looking statement speaks only as of the date on which it is made, and we assume no obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statement whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise, except as required by law.
BA.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Equity Report: BA**  
**Decision Date: 2024-07-01 (all evidence point-in-time before this date)**  

---

### 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory  
- **Revenue**: Q1 2024 revenue was $16.57B, down 24.7% QoQ (from $22.02B in Q4 2023) and down 7.5% YoY (from $17.92B in Q1 2023). TTM revenue totaled $76.44B.  
- **Earnings**: Q1 2024 EPS diluted was -$0.56, an improvement from Q4 2023’s -$0.03 but worse than Q1 2023’s -$0.69. TTM net income was -$2.17B.  
- **Margins**: Q1 2024 gross margin was 11.34% ($1.88B gross profit on $16.57B revenue), down 30.4% QoQ. Operating margin was negative (-0.52%), with operating income of -$86.00M. Net margin was -2.14%. TTM gross profit was $7.68B; operating income was -$710.00M.  
- **Cash-Flow**: No cash-flow data supplied for any period.  
- **Balance Sheet**: Total assets declined from $137.10B (Q4 2022) to $134.48B (Q1 2024). Total debt decreased from $56.79B (Q4 2022) to $47.69B (Q1 2024). Total equity remained negative, ranging from -$17.64B (Q3 2022) to -$17.02B (Q1 2024).  

---

### 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience  
- **Earnings Quality**: Persistent losses over eight quarters, with only one quarter (Q2 2022) showing positive EPS ($0.32). Gross margins volatile, including a negative quarter (Q3 2022: -$822.00M). Operating income was negative in 6 of last 8 quarters. Cash-flow data unavailable, preventing assessment of cash conversion.  
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Total equity is negative (-$17.02B), indicating technical insolvency. Debt-to-assets ratio was 35.5% ($47.69B / $134.48B), but negative equity implies leverage is extreme. Debt decreased by $9.32B over eight quarters. No cash-flow data limits liquidity assessment.  

---

### 3. Valuation versus Company’s Own Supplied History  
- **Price-to-Sales (P/S)**: Latest P/S of 1.459 (as of 2024-06-28).  
- **Other Ratios**: PE, EV/EBIT, FCF yield, P/B, and ROE are unavailable due to negative earnings or missing data.  
- **Net Margin History**: Net margin improved from -6.2% (2023-07-03) to -2.8% (2024-06-28) but remained negative throughout. No positive earnings period exists in the supplied history.  

---

### 4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context vs. Fundamentals  
- **Price Level**: Latest close $182.01 (2024-06-28). 52-week range: $159.70–$267.54; current price is 31.9% below the high.  
- **Quarterly Return**: -5.7% (Q2 2024), -26.4% (Q1 2024), +34.1% (Q4 2023), -7.2% (Q3 2023).  
- **Annual Return**: -13.6% (from $210.72 on 2023-06-28).  
- **Fundamental Context**: Negative earnings and equity persisted despite price volatility. The stock’s annual decline aligns with weak fundamentals, but recent quarterly swings show divergence from stable financial trajectory.  

---

### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside  
- **Upside**: Debt reduction of $9.32B over eight quarters could eventually improve leverage. Gross margin remains positive in most quarters (e.g., 11.34% in Q1 2024), suggesting operational base.  
- **Downside**: Negative equity (-$17.02B) poses insolvency risk. Persistent negative earnings (TTM net income -$2.17B) and lack of cash-flow data obscure ability to fund operations. Revenue volatility and negative operating margins increase financial fragility.  

---

### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion  
The supplied numbers indicate a financially distressed entity: negative equity, sustained losses, and absent cash-flow data. While debt has decreased, balance-sheet resilience is critically weak. Valuation metrics are limited (P/S of 1.459) and cannot be deemed cheap given negative earnings and unclear durability. Price has declined over the past year (-13.6%) but remains volatile without fundamental support. Quantitative risk/reward appears unfavorable until earnings turn positive and balance-sheet strength is demonstrated.  

*Note: All claims derive strictly from supplied data. Cash-flow metrics, detailed segment data, and forward guidance are unavailable.*
BA.qualitative_report:
### **Qualitative Analysis: The Boeing Company (BA)**
**Decision Date:** 2024-07-01
**Evidence Cutoff:** All evidence is point-in-time prior to July 1, 2024.

---

#### **1. Business/Franchise Read from Narrative**
Based on the latest available 10-K filing (2023-01-27), Boeing operates as a major global aerospace firm across four segments:
*   **Commercial Airplanes (BCA):** Develops and produces jet aircraft (737, 777, 787 families) with ongoing development of the 777X and 737 derivatives. Faces intense competition from Airbus and emerging Chinese entrants.
*   **Defense, Space & Security (BDS):** Engages in military aircraft, weapons systems, satellites, and space exploration. Faces strong competition from Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, and others.
*   **Global Services (BGS):** Provides aftermarket services, supply chain, and maintenance for commercial and defense platforms in a highly competitive market.
*   **Boeing Capital (BCC):** Provides financing solutions to customers.

The business is characterized by heavy regulation (FAA, DoD, DOJ), significant supply chain dependencies (noting sole-source suppliers and prior titanium sourcing challenges), and cyclical demand linked to airline health and government budgets.

#### **2. Recent Developments (Dated from Supplied Text)**
*   **Corporate Action / M&A:** On **2024-06-25**, reports indicated Boeing had proposed to acquire Spirit Aerosystems (SPR) for ~$35 per share, seen as an effort to address supply chain and quality control issues within its manufacturing ecosystem.
*   **Contract Win:** On **2024-06-27**, Boeing won a $115 million deal to procure MH-47G helicopters for U.S. Special Operations Command, supporting its defense segment.
*   **Legal & Regulatory Crisis:** As of **2024-05-15**, the Department of Justice (DOJ) found Boeing violated a 2021 settlement related to the 737 Max crashes, potentially exposing the company to criminal prosecution. A separate FAA ultimatum in late February 2024 for Boeing to devise a plan to fix "systemic" quality-control issues had a 90-day deadline set to expire at the end of May 2024.
*   **Corporate Governance:** As of **2024-06-21**, federal prosecutors were evaluating whether to pursue charges or potentially install a federal monitor to oversee Boeing's manufacturing processes.
*   **Financial & Market Sentiment:** On **2024-06-28**, reports noted the stock was down 32% in 2024, with concerns over free cash flow following mixed quarterly results in April. Analyst commentary (2024-06-28) suggested a "big second-half recovery" was possible only if management executed on key operational fixes.

#### **3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
**Risks:**
*   **Unprecedented Legal Exposure:** The confirmed (2024-05-15) DOJ finding that Boeing violated its 2021 deferred prosecution agreement poses a direct risk of criminal prosecution, which could result in massive fines, a potential federal monitor, and severe reputational damage, further eroding trust with regulators and customers.
*   **Regulatory & Operational Disruption:** The concurrent FAA directive (referenced 2024-05-15) addressing "systemic" quality-control issues suggests ongoing regulatory scrutiny that could lead to production restrictions, delays, and increased oversight costs.
*   **Supply Chain Execution:** While the potential Spirit Aerosystems acquisition (2024-06-25) is a strategic move to secure a critical supplier, integration and execution risk is high. The 10-K's general emphasis on sole-source supplier dependencies remains a core vulnerability.
*   **Labor Relations:** The 10-K (2023-01-27) notes a major IAM contract representing 20% of employees is set to expire in September 2024, introducing potential labor disruption risk.

**Potential Offsets:**
*   **Long-term Demand Underpinning:** The 10-K and web context (2024-06-11) highlight strong long-term demand for fuel-efficient aircraft and a robust order backlog.
*   **Defense Segment Stability:** Continued contract wins, such as the $115M helicopter deal (2024-06-27), provide steady revenue streams.
*   **Potential Strategic Remedy:** Successfully executing the Spirit Aerosystems acquisition could provide long-term operational and quality benefits.

#### **4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Negative.**

The qualitative picture as of early July 2024 is dominated by acute, material risks that outweigh fundamental franchise strengths. The confirmed violation of the 2021 DOJ settlement (2024-05-15) and the looming FAA quality-control deadline represent existential legal and regulatory threats that could result in criminal charges, operational oversight, and lasting reputational harm. While the company retains a strong position in a duopoly market with long-term demand tailwinds, near-term uncertainty is exceptionally high due to these unresolved crises. The potential acquisition of Spirit Aerosystems (2024-06-25) is a strategic reactive measure, not a resolution to the core governance and quality culture failures highlighted by the DOJ and FAA actions. The negative trajectory in the stock price (2024-06-28) reflects this severe impairment of investor confidence.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Q1 2024 EPS diluted was -$0.56, an improvement from Q4 2023's -$0.03' — this is misleading. While -$0.56 is a smaller absolute loss than -$0.03, calling it an 'improvement' is incorrect; -$0.03 is closer to breakeven and thus the better result. The raw data shows Q4 2023 EPS of -0.03 was actually the best quarter in the trailing period, not Q1 2024.
corrections.required_action: Correct the directional characterization: Q4 2023 (-$0.03) was the better quarter; Q1 2024 (-$0.56) was a deterioration from that level.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Operating income was negative in 6 of last 8 quarters' — the raw data shows negative operating income in 6 of 8 quarters (Q1 2024, Q3 2023, Q2 2023, Q1 2023, Q4 2022, Q3 2022) and positive in Q4 2023 ($283M) and Q2 2022 ($774M). This is numerically correct. However, the report also says 'only one quarter (Q2 2022) showing positive EPS' — the raw data confirms only Q2 2022 had positive EPS (0.32), so this is accurate. No correction needed on this specific count, but the report should note Q4 2023 had positive operating income ($283M) alongside negative EPS (-$0.03), which is an important earnings quality signal (non-operating charges depressing net income) that is omitted.
corrections.required_action: Add a note that Q4 2023 had positive operating income ($283M) but negative net income (-$30M), indicating non-operating items (likely interest expense or other below-the-line charges) are a material drag on earnings — this is relevant for earnings quality assessment.

corrections.issue: Qual report references 'the 10-K's general emphasis on sole-source supplier dependencies' and 'prior titanium sourcing challenges' — the 10-K text does mention sole-source suppliers and titanium sourcing (Russia suspension in 2022), but the qual report frames this as a current 'core vulnerability' without noting the 10-K also states 'This has not disrupted our operations as we have been able to use inventory on hand and identify alternative sources.' The omission of this mitigating language from the 10-K skews the risk presentation.
corrections.required_action: Include the 10-K's own mitigation language regarding titanium sourcing to provide balanced risk assessment.

unsupported_claims: Quant report: 'Q1 2024 EPS diluted was -$0.56, an improvement from Q4 2023's -$0.03' — the raw data shows Q4 2023 EPS of -0.03 was closer to breakeven; characterizing -0.56 as an 'improvement' over -0.03 is a directional error.

Qual report: 'prior titanium sourcing challenges' presented as ongoing vulnerability — the 10-K explicitly states the Russia titanium suspension 'has not disrupted our operations' and alternative sources were identified, which is omitted from the qual report.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Boeing faces an acute, unresolved legal and regulatory crisis—confirmed DOJ settlement violation (2024-05-15) and FAA quality-control ultimatum—that creates existential near-term risk, compounded by persistent negative equity (-$17.02B), sustained losses (TTM net income -$2.17B), and deteriorating Q1 2024 results (EPS -$0.56, operating margin -0.52%). While the duopoly franchise and $520B backlog provide a long-term structural floor, the risk-adjusted reward over the next year is clearly unfavorable given the severity of governance failures and financial fragility.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Boeing faces an acute, unresolved legal and regulatory crisis—confirmed DOJ settlement violation (2024-05-15) and FAA quality-control ultimatum—that creates existential near-term risk, compounded by persistent negative equity (-$17.02B), sustained losses (TTM net income -$2.17B), and deteriorating Q1 2024 results (EPS -$0.56, operating margin -0.52%). While the duopoly franchise and $520B backlog provide a long-term structural floor, the risk-adjusted reward over the next year is clearly unfavorable given the severity of governance failures and financial fragility.
key_factors: Legal and regulatory crisis is existential: DOJ confirmed Boeing violated its 2021 deferred prosecution agreement (2024-05-15), exposing the company to potential criminal prosecution, massive fines, and a federal monitor; concurrent FAA directive (2024-05-15) addressing 'systemic' quality-control issues with a 90-day deadline compounds operational uncertainty.

Balance sheet remains critically impaired: negative equity of -$17.02B (Q1 2024), TTM net loss of -$2.17B, and only one quarter of positive EPS (Q2 2022) in the trailing eight-quarter period; Q1 2024 EPS of -$0.56 represents a deterioration from Q4 2023's -$0.03, which was the closest to breakeven.

Revenue and margins deteriorating sharply: Q1 2024 revenue of $16.57B was down 24.7% QoQ and 7.5% YoY, with gross margin falling 30.4% QoQ to 11.34% and operating margin turning negative (-0.52%), reflecting the operational disruption from the 737 MAX 9 door plug incident and subsequent production restrictions.

Stock has already declined 32% YTD (as of 2024-06-28) and trades at $182.01, 31.9% below its 52-week high, but P/S of 1.459 is not compellingly cheap given negative earnings, absent cash-flow data, and the magnitude of unresolved legal/regulatory overhang.

ITEM 1.    Business
OVERVIEW
Visa is one of the world's leaders in digital payments. Our purpose is to uplift everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid. We facilitate global commerce and money movement across more than 200 countries and territories among a global set of consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities through innovative technologies.
Since Visa's early days in 1958, we have been in the business of facilitating payments between consumers and businesses. We are focused on extending, enhancing and investing in our proprietary advanced transaction processing network, VisaNet, to offer a single connection point for facilitating payment transactions to multiple endpoints through various form factors. As a network of networks enabling global movement of money through all available networks, we are working to provide payment solutions and services for everyone, everywhere. Through our network, we offer products, solutions and services that facilitate secure, reliable and efficient money movement for participants in the ecosystem.
- We facilitate secure, reliable and efficient money movement among consumers, issuing and acquiring financial institutions and merchants. We have traditionally referred to this structure as the "four-party" model. Please see Our Core Business discussion below. As the payments ecosystem continues to evolve, we have broadened this model to include digital banks, digital wallets and a range of financial technology companies (fintechs), governments and non-governmental organizations (NGOs). We provide transaction processing services (primarily authorization, clearing and settlement) to our financial institution and merchant clients through VisaNet. During fiscal year 2023, 276 billion payments and cash transactions with Visa's brand were processed by Visa or other networks, equating to an average of 757 million transactions per day. Of the 276 billion total transactions, 213 billion were processed by Visa.

Open Banking
In March 2022, Visa acquired Tink AB, an open banking platform, to catalyze fintech innovation and accelerate the development and adoption of open banking securely and at scale. Visa's open banking capabilities range from data access use cases, such as account verification, balance check and personal finance management, to payment initiation capabilities, such as account-to-account transactions and merchant payments. These capabilities can help our partner businesses deliver valuable services to their customers.
Advisory Services
Visa Consulting and Analytics (VCA) is the payments consulting advisory arm of Visa. The combination of our deep payments expertise, proprietary analytical models applied to a breadth of data and our economic intelligence allows us to identify actionable insights, make recommendations and help implement solutions that can drive better business decisions and measurable outcomes for clients. VCA offers consulting services for issuers, acquirers, merchants, fintechs and other partners, spanning the entire customer journey from acquisition to retention. Further, VCA Managed Services, our dedicated execution arm within the consulting division, is being increasingly utilized by clients to implement our recommendations and wider value added services product enablement.

We are fortifying the key foundations of our business model, which consist of becoming a network of networks, our technology platforms, security, brand and talent.

Network of Networks
Our network of networks strategy means moving money to all endpoints and to all form factors, using all available networks and being a single connection point for our partners; and providing our value added services on all transactions, no matter the network. The key component of our network of networks strategy is interoperability. We are opening up our network and increasingly using other networks to reach accounts we could not otherwise reach and enabling new types of money movement. Visa B2B Connect, Visa Direct, and Visa+ are examples of our strategy.
Technology Platforms
Visa's leading technology platforms comprise software, hardware, data centers and a large telecommunications infrastructure. Visa's four data centers are a critical part of our global processing environment and have a high redundancy of network connectivity, power and cooling designed to provide continuous availability of systems. Together, these systems deliver the secure, convenient and reliable service that our clients and consumers expect from the Visa brand.
Security
Our in-depth, multi-layer security approach includes a formal program to devalue sensitive and/or personal data through various cryptographic means; embedded security in the software development lifecycle; identity and access management controls to protect against unauthorized access; and advanced cyber detection and response capabilities. We deploy security tools that help keep our clients and consumers safe. We also invest significantly in our comprehensive approach to cybersecurity. We deploy security technologies to protect data confidentiality, the integrity of our network and service availability to strengthen our core cybersecurity capabilities to minimize risk. Our

Brand
Visa's strong brand helps deliver added value to our clients and their customers, financial institutions, merchants and partners through compelling brand expressions, a wide range of products and services as well as innovative brand and marketing efforts. In line with our commitment to an expansive and diverse range of partnerships for the benefit of our stakeholders, Visa is a sponsor of top entertainment and sports events including the FIFA Women's World Cup 2023TM, the Olympic and Paralympic Games, and the Super Bowl.
Talent
Attracting, developing and advancing the best talent globally is critical to our continued success. This year we grew our total workforce from approximately 26,500 in fiscal year 2022 to approximately 28,800 employees in fiscal year 2023, an increase of 9 percent year over year. Voluntary workforce turnover (rolling 12-month attrition) was 6 percent as of September 30, 2023. Visa employees are located in more than 80 countries and territories, with 55 percent located outside the U.S. At the end of fiscal year 2023, Visa's global workforce was 58 percent men and 42 percent women, and women represented 36 percent of Visa's leadership (defined as vice president level and above). In the U.S., ethnicity of our workforce was 42 percent Asian, 8 percent Black, 13 percent Hispanic, 3 percent Other and 35 percent White. For our U.S. leadership, the breakdown was 18 percent Asian, 6 percent Black, 13 percent Hispanic, 3 percent Other and 60 percent White.
Given Visa's ambitious growth agenda and efforts to achieve our purpose, we have focused on enhancing our employees' expertise across our business. This includes an enhanced development program for our senior leaders and a formal technology apprenticeship program to help us broaden and strengthen our talent channels and pipelines. We have also committed to providing employees with the tools they need to do their work more quickly and easily, including an artificial intelligence or AI-driven portal with a searchable knowledge base to create customized results and bespoke solutions. We enhanced our mental well-being and retirement benefits, which is reflective of our key priority to take care of our employees.
We also are dedicated to ensuring that employees feel valued in their day-to-day work. During our global employee engagement survey last year, we learned that our employees wanted more opportunities to recognize and be recognized, in more informal ways. In response, Visa developed a program that better enabled employees to provide peer-to-peer recognition for each other's contributions. Using UPLIFT, Visa's new recognition platform, employees can celebrate their peers' achievements, send e-cards to celebrate the employee journey (from welcoming new hires to recognizing service anniversaries), use an automated internal networking tool that matches employees based on smart algorithms, and more. Importantly, all our recognition categories are grounded in behaviors that reflect our employee value proposition or Visa's Leadership Principles – further reinforcing that at Visa, it is not only about what you achieve, but how you do it. **Quantitative Report: Visa (V) as of 2024-07-01**  
*Evidence is point-in-time prior to 2024-07-01. Cash-flow and key valuation multiples are unavailable.*

---

### 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory
- **Revenue**: TTM revenue = $34.14B. Quarterly revenue grew consistently from $7.28B (Q3 2022) to $8.78B (Q2 2024). YoY growth = +9.9% (Q2 2024 vs. Q2 2023). QoQ growth = +1.6%.
- **Net Income**: TTM net income = $18.39B. YoY growth = +9.5% (Q2 2024: $4.66B vs. $4.26B). QoQ change = -4.6% (from Q1 2024’s $4.89B).
- **Operating Income**: Latest quarter = $5.35B; TTM = $21.88B.
- **Margins**: As of 2024-06-28: net margin = 0.539, operating margin = 0.641. Net margin expanded from 0.510 one year prior.
- **Cash-Flow**: All quarterly cash-flow data is unavailable.
- **Balance Sheet**: Total assets = $92.40B, total equity = $40.48B. Debt-to-equity ratio (total liabilities/equity) = 1.28 (2024-03-31), improved from 1.41 in Q3 2022. Equity has grown steadily from $35.48B to $40.48B over eight quarters.

### 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience
- **Earnings Quality**: High and improving profitability metrics (net margin 0.539, ROE 0.454). The QoQ net income decline (-4.6%) contrasts with stable YoY growth, but margin expansion indicates consistent operational leverage. Cash-flow generation is not quantifiable from supplied data.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Leverage ratio declining (from 1.41 to 1.28 over two years). Equity base has grown by 14.1% since Q3 2022, supported by retained earnings.

### 3. Valuation Versus Own Supplied History
- **Valuation Multiples**: PE, EV/EBIT, P/B, and FCF yield are unavailable for assessment.
- **Profitability History**: Net margin increased from 0.510 (2023-07-03) to 0.539 (2024-06-28). ROE rose from 0.409 to 0.454 over the same period. No price-multiple history is supplied to gauge relative valuation.

### 4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context
- **Price**: Close of $262.47 (2024-06-28). 52-week range: $227.68 to $290.96.
- **Returns**: Quarterly return (from 2024-03-28) = -6.0%. Annual return (from 2023-06-28) = +15.1%.
- **Fundamental Path vs. Price**: Annual price return (+15.1%) is broadly in line with YoY revenue growth (+9.9%) and net income growth (+9.5%). The recent quarterly price decline (-6.0%) occurred alongside positive YoY fundamentals but a QoQ earnings dip.

### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside
- **Upside**: Consistent quarterly revenue expansion, high profitability (net margin >53%), and improving balance-sheet leverage.
- **Downside**: QoQ earnings volatility (-4.6% net income), absence of cash-flow data limits assessment of quality, and lack of valuation multiples prevents determination of whether price reflects fundamentals.

### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion
Fundamental trajectory is positive with steady revenue growth, high margins, and deleveraging. However, the inability to quantify cash flow or assess valuation multiples (PE, EV/EBIT, P/B) materially limits the risk/reward assessment. The annual price return aligns with earnings growth, but the recent quarterly drawdown warrants caution without clearer valuation context.
V.qualitative_report:
### Qualitative Assessment Report: Visa (V)
**Decision Date:** 2024-07-01

**1. Business/Franchise Read from Narrative**
Visa operates as a global leader in digital payments, facilitating money movement across more than 200 countries and territories. Its business model centers on its proprietary VisaNet processing network, acting as a central hub in a "four-party" system connecting consumers, merchants, financial institutions, and increasingly, fintechs and digital wallets. The company does not issue credit or set fees for consumers; instead, it earns revenue from service, data processing, and international transaction fees, net of incentives paid to clients. A core strategic pillar is evolving into a "network of networks" to enable all forms of money movement (C2B, P2P, B2B, etc.) through a single connection point, leveraging its scale (4.3 billion payment credentials, 130 million merchant locations) and strong brand.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Product & Partnership (2024-06-28):** Visa and Amazon launched a new Buy Now, Pay Later (BNPL) solution for Canadian consumers, aimed at increasing transaction volumes and platform engagement.
*   **Product Innovation (2024-06-27):** Visa introduced a new card replacement service, timed to coincide with summer travel demand for secure payment solutions.
*   **Legal/Regulatory (2024-06-28):** A U.S. federal judge rejected a proposed $30 billion settlement between Visa, Mastercard, and merchants regarding credit card interchange fees. The case stems from a 2005 antitrust lawsuit.
*   **Legal/Regulatory (2024-05-29):** Visa and Mastercard agreed to pay $197 million to settle a separate consumer class-action lawsuit over ATM access fees.
*   **Corporate Action (2024-06-14):** Visa enhanced its SavingsEdge program to offer more discounts to small business cardholders, aiming to boost adoption of Visa-branded products.
*   **Financial Performance (noted in web context, Q1 2024):** Growth in "new flows" (commercial cards, Visa Direct) drove a 14% revenue increase in that segment, with overall earnings per share growing 11% year-over-year.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Ongoing Legal and Regulatory Uncertainty.** The rejection of the massive interchange fee settlement (2024-06-28) extends a long-running legal overhang, creating uncertainty about potential future liability or mandated fee structure changes. Settlements like the $197M ATM fee case (2024-05-29) demonstrate continued costs from litigation.
    *   **Offset:** The company's business model is designed to be somewhat insulated from direct interchange fee setting. The 10-K narrative emphasizes that Visa's fees are set independently from interchange and that the potential impact of known regulations (e.g., Reg II) is considered modest. Furthermore, growth in areas like value-added services and new flows can diversify revenue away from traditional interchange-dependent streams.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral to Cautiously Positive.**
*   **Positive Factors:** Visa maintains a dominant, globally scaled franchise with a clear strategic focus on growth in new flows and value-added services, which are showing strong momentum. Recent product launches (BNPL, card replacement) demonstrate active innovation to capture transaction volume.
*   **Negative Factors:** The core business faces persistent, material legal risk from antitrust litigation, as highlighted by the recent settlement rejection. This introduces uncertainty and potential future costs.
*   **Overall:** The evidence confirms a robust and growing business with multiple expansion levers. However, the unresolved major legal challenge represents a significant qualitative risk that offsets some of the positive operational momentum, leading to a balanced assessment.

A 15.1% price return vs ~10% earnings growth implies P/E multiple expansion of roughly 5%, which is not 'broadly in line' — it suggests the market is pricing in some multiple expansion or future growth acceleration. The report should note this gap rather than characterizing them as aligned.

pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Visa remains an elite franchise with durable double-digit earnings growth and best-in-class profitability, but the stock's recent pullback (-6.0% quarterly) reflects valuation compression after a period of multiple expansion, and the rejection of the $30B merchant settlement extends legal overhang. The risk/reward is balanced at current levels.
key_factors: TTM net income of $18.39B with YoY growth of +9.5% and quarterly revenue growth of +9.9% YoY to $8.78B, demonstrating durable high-single-digit to low-double-digit earnings growth.
Net margin expanded to 53.9% (up from 51.0% a year prior) and ROE increased to 45.4%, reflecting exceptional operational leverage and capital efficiency.
Annual price return of +15.1% versus +9.5% net income growth implies ~5pp of multiple expansion, leaving limited valuation upside without earnings acceleration.

U.S. federal judge rejected the proposed $30B merchant interchange fee settlement (2024-06-28), extending a material legal overhang and creating uncertainty about future liability or mandated fee changes.

MCD.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: MCD as of 2024-07-01**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
*   **Revenue & Earnings:** TTM revenue is $25.76B. In the latest quarter (Q1 2024), revenue was $6.17B, a +4.6% YoY increase from $5.90B in Q1 2023. However, it represents a -3.7% QoQ decline from Q4 2023's $6.41B. TTM net income is $8.60B. Q1 2024 EPS diluted was $2.66, up +8.6% YoY ($2.45) but down -5.0% QoQ ($2.80).
*   **Margins:** Q1 2024 gross profit was $5.54B (gross margin ~89.8%). Operating income was $2.74B (operating margin ~44.4%). Net income was $1.93B (net margin ~31.3%). Margins are stable, with TTM operating income of $11.85B.
*   **Cash Flow:** Not available for any period in the lookback window.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total assets were $53.51B as of 2024-03-31. Total liabilities were $4.83B, resulting in negative shareholder equity of **-$4.83B**. This negative equity position is consistent across the entire 8-quarter history provided.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** Assessment is limited by the absence of cash flow data. Net margins are consistently high (~31-33%). The trajectory shows QoQ volatility in EPS but YoY growth.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The balance sheet is characterized by persistent negative equity (e.g., -$4.83B in Q1 2024, -$4.71B in Q4 2023, -$6.00B in Q4 2022). Total debt was $39.30B in Q4 2023. This indicates a highly leveraged capital structure.

**3. Valuation Versus Supplied History**
*   Latest valuation ratios (as of 2024-06-28) show **pe_diluted=0** and **earnings_yield=46.47K**, which appear anomalous or reflect a data error. The sparse history shows a normal range for **pe_diluted** over the past year between **22.946** (2023-10-09) and **31.541** (2023-07-03).
*   **Net_margin** has been stable, ranging from **0.294** to **0.334** over the same period. The latest value is **0.334**.

**4. Price Level & Return Context**
*   **Price Level:** The stock closed at **$254.84** on 2024-06-28, near the 365-day low of **$245.73** and well below the high of **$302.39**.
*   **Quarterly/Annual Return:** The quarterly price delta (from 2024-03-28) is **-9.6%**. The annual price delta (from 2023-06-28) is **-12.6%**. The price has declined over both periods despite YoY growth in revenue and EPS.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Strongest Upside:** Consistent YoY revenue and earnings growth with high, stable operating margins (~44.4%). TTM operating income of **$11.85B** demonstrates significant profitability.
*   **Strongest Downside:** The balance sheet exhibits persistent negative equity (most recently **-$4.83B**). High leverage (total debt of **$39.30B** in Q4 2023) poses financial risk, especially without cash flow data to confirm debt service capacity.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The fundamental path shows growing revenue and earnings with robust margins. However, the price has significantly underperformed these fundamentals over the past year (-12.6% annual return). This disconnect likely reflects market concern over the extreme leverage (negative equity) and the absence of cash flow evidence to validate earnings quality. The quantitative profile presents a conflict between operational profitability and balance-sheet fragility. Valuation is unclear due to anomalous recent PE data, but the price near 52-week lows may be incorporating the balance-sheet risk. The risk/reward is contingent on the market's perception of the durability of earnings in the face of high leverage.

**Note:** Key evidence gaps include all cash flow data and clarifying the anomalous latest PE_diluted of 0.
MCD.qualitative_report:
# McDonald's Corporation (MCD) — Qualitative Assessment
**Decision Date: 2024-07-01 | Evidence window: 2024-05-12 to 2024-06-30**

---

## 1. Business / Franchise Read

McDonald's is a global quick-service restaurant giant operating primarily through a franchise-driven model. Its competitive advantages include an iconic brand, an extensive global footprint, and an efficient franchising system. The company has a significant presence in emerging markets (Asia, Africa, Latin America) that present growth runway tied to urbanization and rising disposable incomes. Digital engagement is a strategic pillar: the loyalty program had approximately **150 million active global users** as of the evidence window (2024-05-12, clickz.com), and the company is investing in marketing that resonates with younger demographics. Web sources characterize the franchise as having "many positive tailwinds" in 2024, including planned price reductions and loyalty-driven comparable sales growth (2024-06-04, nasdaq.com).

---

## 2. Recent Developments (Dated)

| Date | Development |
|---|---|
| **2024-06-30** | MCD highlighted as a Dividend Aristocrat; press coverage frames it as a reliable long-term income holding, though some caution that valuations may be elevated relative to yields (news). |
| **2024-06-24** | MCD closed at $260.38, up +0.38% on a day the broader market slipped — suggesting relative defensive strength (news). |
| **2024-06-24** | Goldman Sachs flagged election/tariff risk for internationally-exposed U.S. stocks. MCD, with heavy overseas revenue, is implicitly in this category (news). |
| **2024-06-12** | PFAS packaging concerns re-surfaced, though reporting noted FDA approval of PFAS in food packaging and no current regulatory violation (thedailyeconomy.org). |
| **2024-06-11 – 2024-06-05** | EU General Court partially annulled McDonald's "Big Mac" trademark for poultry products, ruling against the company in a long-running dispute. McDonald's lost the right to the Big Mac mark for chicken sandwiches in the EU. |
| **2024-06-04** | Nasdaq.com article cited company plans to lower prices and a growing loyalty rewards program as tangible catalysts for comparable sales. |
| **2024-05-23** | A large franchisee filed a lawsuit alleging corporate ouster, claiming McDonald's enforced revised National Franchising Standards against him. McDonald's countered that the franchisee violated those standards. |
| **2024-05-12** | Marketing/digital strategy article highlighted the 150-million-user loyalty program and pivot toward digital engagement as a growth cornerstone credible qualifier. |

---

## 3. Material Qualitative Risks & Offsets

**Risks:**

- **Macro / tariff / election exposure (2024-06-24):** Goldman Sachs warns that U.S. election outcomes and tariff prospects threaten internationally-exposed companies. MCD's heavy non-U.S. revenue profile makes it vulnerable to dollar strength and trade-policy disruption. *Confirmed in supplied text.*
- **Trademark erosion in EU (2024-06-05):** Partial loss of the Big Mac trademark for poultry products in Europe weakens brand-control in a key market. *Confirmed in supplied text.*
- **Franchisee litigation (2024-05-23):** A large franchisee suing McDonald's over alleged ouster under revised National Franchising Standards signals potential friction in the franchise system, which is core to the business model. *Confirmed in supplied text.*
- **Valuation / yield caution (2024-06-30):** Multiple articles characterize the stock as potentially overvalued for dividend buyers, with low yields relative to price. Broad market commentary (2024-06-28, 2024-06-21) flags elevated S&P 500 P/E and recession risk. *Inference: MCD is not immune to a general de-rating.*
- **Food safety / PFAS liability (2024-06-04, 2024-06-12):** Ongoing legal and reputational risk from packaging chemicals and food-safety lawsuits. Currently mitigated by FDA approval status but remains a litigation tail-risk. *Confirmed in supplied text.*

**Offsets:**

- **Loyalty & digital flywheel (2024-05-12, 2024-06-04):** 150 million active loyalty users and growing, driving comparable sales domestically and internationally. *Confirmed in supplied text.*
- **Pricing flexibility / value positioning (2024-05-22, 2024-06-04):** Company plans to lower prices while tapping youth-oriented marketing; value platforms offset consumer sensitivity to prior menu-price inflation. *Confirmed in supplied text.*
- **Emerging-market runway (2024-06-06):** Untapped growth in Asia, Africa, and Latin America. *Partially inferred — general market commentary rather than company-specific guidance.*
- **Defensive profile:** Market-relative outperformance on a down day (2024-06-24) and consistent Dividend Aristocrat status support the case for stability in uncertain macro conditions. *Confirmed in supplied text.*

---

## 4. Qualitative Assessment: **Neutral-to-Slightly-Positive**

**Rationale:** The franchise remains high-quality — iconic brand, massive loyalty base, global scale, and optionality in emerging markets. Digital and pricing strategies appear to be actively managed catalysts. However, the evidence window reveals non-trivial headwinds: EU trademark erosion, franchisee-system litigation risk, and meaningful international macro/governance exposure flagged by Goldman Sachs. No company-sourced 10-K business section was available for deeper financial validation. The balance of catalysts and risks suggests a **neutral-to-slightly-positive** qualitative stance — the durable franchise offsets near-term pressures, but the absence of fresh company-level guidance and the accumulation of legal/regulatory frictions temper conviction.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Total liabilities were $4.83B' for Q1 2024, but the raw balance sheet shows total_liabilities=4.83B for 2024-03-31 while total_equity=-4.83B. The report correctly notes negative equity of -$4.83B but the liability figure appears to match the equity figure exactly, suggesting a possible data anomaly where total_liabilities may be misreported as equal to the absolute value of negative equity rather than reflecting actual liabilities. The report should flag this data inconsistency more explicitly rather than presenting the $4.83B liability figure at face value.
corrections.required_action: Note that the Q1 2024 balance sheet data (total_liabilities=4.83B matching total_equity=-4.83B exactly) may reflect a data reporting anomaly. The Q4 2023 and Q4 2022 periods show total_liabilities of $60.85B and $56.44B respectively, which are far larger and more consistent with MCD's known leverage. The report should caveat the Q1 2024 liability figure rather than using it to characterize the capital structure.

corrections.issue: The quant report's 'Strongest Downside' section cites 'total debt of $39.30B in Q4 2023' as evidence of high leverage. This figure is confirmed in the raw balance sheet for 2023-12-31. However, the report does not note that Q1 2024 balance sheet data lacks a total_debt figure, so the most recent quarter's debt level is unknown from the supplied evidence.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: MCD presents a classic quality-at-a-discount setup: the stock is near 52-week lows ($254.84 vs. high of $302.39) with stable YoY revenue growth (+4.6%) and robust operating margins (~44.4%), but the persistent negative equity (-$4.83B), high leverage ($39.3B debt as of Q4 2023), and data quality issues around the latest PE reading prevent a confident bullish call. The balance between operational resilience and structural balance-sheet fragility warrants a near-neutral stance with mild upside bias.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: MCD presents a classic quality-at-a-discount setup: the stock is near 52-week lows ($254.84 vs. high of $302.39) with stable YoY revenue growth (+4.6%) and robust operating margins (~44.4%), but the persistent negative equity (-$4.83B), high leverage ($39.3B debt as of Q4 2023), and data quality issues around the latest PE reading prevent a confident bullish call. The balance between operational resilience and structural balance-sheet fragility warrants a near-neutral stance with mild upside bias.
key_factors: Stock near 52-week low at $254.84 (vs. $302.39 high) with -12.6% annual price decline despite +4.6% YoY revenue growth and +8.6% YoY EPS growth in Q1 2024, suggesting the market has already priced in significant balance-sheet and macro risk.

Persistent negative shareholder equity of -$4.83B (Q1 2024) and total debt of $39.3B (Q4 2023) create structural fragility; the Q1 2024 liability figure of $4.83B appears anomalous (matching equity exactly) versus prior quarters ($60.85B in Q4 2023), introducing data uncertainty.

Loyalty program with ~150 million active global users (2024-05-12) and planned price reductions (2024-06-04) provide tangible near-term catalysts for comparable sales growth, partially offsetting macro headwinds.

EU Big Mac trademark partial loss for poultry (2024-06-05), franchisee litigation (2024-05-23), and Goldman Sachs tariff/election risk warning for internationally-exposed stocks (2024-06-24) represent non-trivial qualitative headwinds.

Note About Forward-Looking Statements
This report includes estimates, projections, statements relating to our business plans, objectives, and expected operating results that are "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. These forward-looking statements generally are identified by the words "believe," "project," "expect," "anticipate," "estimate," "intend," "strategy," "future," "opportunity," "plan," "may," "should," "will," "would," "will be," "will continue," "will likely result," and similar expressions. We describe risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results and events to differ materially in "Risk Factors," "Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations," and "Quantitative and Qualitative Disclosures about Market Risk" (Part II, Item 7A of this Form 10-K). Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on forward-looking statements, which speak only as of the date they are made. We undertake no obligation to update or revise publicly any forward-looking statements, whether because of new information, future events, or otherwise.
3
Item 1
ITEM 1. BUSINESS
GENERAL
Embracing Our Future
Microsoft is a technology company whose mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. We strive to create local opportunity, growth, and impact in every country around the world. We are creating the platforms and tools, powered by artificial intelligence ("AI"), that deliver better, faster, and more effective solutions to support small and large business competitiveness, improve educational and health outcomes, grow public-sector efficiency,  and empower human ingenuity. From infrastructure and data, to business applications and collaboration, we provide unique, differentiated value to customers.
In a world of increasing economic complexity, AI has the power to revolutionize many types of work. Microsoft is now innovating and expanding our portfolio with AI capabilities to hel
…[truncated 19361 chars]…
ing, and inclusive work environment that enables our global employees to create products and services that further our mission.
8
Item 1
As of June 30, 2023, we employed approximately 221,000 people on a full-time basis, 120,000 in the U.S. and 101,000 internationally. Of the total employed people, 89,000 were in operations, including manufacturing, distribution, product support, and consulting services; 72,000 were in product research and development; 45,000 were in sales and marketing; and 15,000 were in general and administration. Certain employees are subject to collective bargaining agreements.
Our Culture
Microsoft's culture is grounded in growth mindset. This means everyone is on a continuous journey to learn and grow, operating as one company instead of multiple siloed businesses.
Our employee listening systems enable us to gather feedback directly from our workforce to inform our programs and employee needs globally. Employees participate in our Employee Signals surveys, which cover a variety of topics such as thriving, inclusion, team culture, wellbeing, and learning and development. We also collect Daily Signals employee survey responses, giving us real-time insights into ways we can support our employees. In addition to Employee Signals and Daily Signals surveys, we gain insights through onboarding, exit surveys, internal Viva Engage channels, employee Q&A sessions, and our internal AskHR Service support.
Diversity and inclusion are core to our business model, and we hold ourselves accountable for driving global systemic change in our workforce and creating an inclusive work environment. We support multiple highly active Employee Resource Groups for women, families, racial and ethnic minorities, military, people with disabilities, and employees who identify as LGBTQIA+, where employees can go for support, networking, and community-building. As described in our 2022 Proxy Statement, annual performance and compensation reviews of our senior leadership team include an evaluation of their contributions to employee culture and diversity. To ensure accountability over time, we publicly disclose our progress on a multitude of workforce metrics including:
- Detailed breakdowns of gender, racial, and ethnic minority representation in our employee population, with data by job types, levels, and segments of our business.
- Our EEO-1 report (equal employment opportunity).
- Disability representation.
- Pay equity (see details below).
Total Rewards and Pay Equity
We develop dynamic, sustainable, market-driven, and strategic programs with the goal of providing a highly differentiated portfolio to attract, reward, and retain top talent and enable our employees to thrive. These programs reinforce our culture and values such as collaboration and growth mindset. Managers evaluate and recommend rewards based on, for example, how well we leverage the work of others and contribute to the success of our colleagues. We monitor pay equity and career progress across multiple dimensions. Our total compensation opportunity is highly differentiated and is market competitive.
In order to manage our costs in a dynamic, competitive environment, in fiscal year 2023 we announced that base salaries of salaried employees would remain at fiscal year 2022 levels. Pay increases continue to be available for rewards-eligible hourly and equivalent employees. We will continue our practice of investing in stock for all rewards-eligible employees, salaried and hourly, and investing in bonuses for all eligible employees.
9
Item 1
Since 2016, we have reported on pay equity as part of our annual Diversity and Inclusion report. In 2022, we reported that all racial and ethnic minority employees in the U.S. combined earn $1.008 for every $1.000 earned by their white counterparts, that women in the U.S. earn $1.007 for every $1.000 earned by their counterparts who are men, and that women outside the U.S. earn $1.002 for every $1.000 earned by their counterparts outside the U.S. who are men. In this year's report, we again expanded our pay equity data beyond the U.S. to report on 61 additional countries (up from 12 last year), representing 99.8% of our global Microsoft workforce.
In addition, we began reporting on unadjusted median pay in our annual report, comparing total pay amounts for all employees regardless of factors such as job title, level, or tenure. For employees who are eligible for rewards, the analysis showed that total pay for women is 89.6% of total pay for men in the U.S. and 86.2% outside of the U.S., and total pay for racial and ethnic minorities in the U.S. is 89.9% of total pay for white employees. As we continue to increase representation for women and racial and ethnic minorities at more senior levels, and continue to ensure pay equity for all, the gap between the medians will reduce.
Our intended result is a global performance and development approach that fosters our culture, and competitive compensation that ensures equitable pay by role while supporting pay for performance.
Wellbeing and Hybrid Work
Microsoft is committed to supporting our employees' wellbeing while they are at work and in their personal lives. We have invested significantly in wellbeing, and offer a differentiated benefits package which includes many physical, emotional, and financial wellness programs including counseling through the Microsoft CARES Employee Assistance Program, mental wellbeing support, flexible fitness benefits, disability accommodations, savings and investment tools, adoption assistance, and back-up care for children and elders. Finally, our Occupational Health and Safety program helps ensure employees can stay safe while they are working.
We introduced Hybrid Workplace Flexibility Guidance to better support leaders, managers, and employees in hybrid work scenarios. Our ongoing survey data shows that 93% of employees value the flexibility related to work location, work site, and work hours, and 78% are satisfied with the quality of connection with co-workers. There is no one-size-fits-all approach to flexible work at Microsoft. As a company, we will continue to leverage data and research to inform decision making, balancing the needs of business, team, and individual.
Learning and Development
We offer a range of learning opportunities, including personalized opportunities on our internal and external learning portals, in-classroom learning, required learning on compliance and company culture, on-the-job advancement opportunities, and manager coaching. We also provide customized manager learning, new employee orientation, and tools for operating in a flexible hybrid work environment.
All Microsoft employees globally access our single Viva Learning tool for both required and personal choice learning. This includes courses focused on our core principles and compliance matters, such as Business Conduct, Privacy, Security Foundations, and Harassment Prevention. We also deliver skills training for employees based on their profession and role discipline.
We have over 27,000 people managers, all of whom must complete between 20-33 hours of compulsory training on leadership and management and are assigned additional targeted training on an ongoing basis related to people management, compliance, and culture.
10
Item 1
OPERATING SEGMENTS
We operate our business and report our financial performance using three segments: Productivity and Business Processes, Intelligent Cloud, and More Personal Computing. Our segments provide management with a comprehensive financial view of our key businesses. The segments enable the alignment of strategies and objectives across the development, sales, marketing, and services organizations, and they provide a framework for timely and rational allocation of resources within businesses.
Additional information on our operating segments and geographic and product information is contained in Note 19 – Segment Info

MSFT.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: MSFT (Decision Date: 2024-07-01)**

1. **Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**  
   - **Revenue:** TTM revenue of $236.58B. Latest quarterly revenue (Q3 2024) of $61.86B, YoY growth +17.0%, but QoQ decline of -0.3%.  
   - **Earnings:** TTM net income of $86.18B. Latest EPS diluted of $2.94, YoY growth +20.0%, QoQ +0.3%.  
   - **Margins:** As of 2024-06-28, gross margin 0.699, operating margin 0.447, net margin 0.364. Quarterly gross profit grew +2.3% QoQ to $43.35B.  
   - **Cash-Flow:** Free cash flow data is unavailable for all periods provided.  
   - **Balance-Sheet:** As of 2024-03-31, total assets $484.27B, total equity $253.15B, total debt $44.91B, cash $80.02B. Equity has grown from $166.54B in Q4 2022.

2. **Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**  
   - **Earnings Quality:** Net income has consistently increased from $16.74B (Q4 2022) to $21.94B (Q3 2024), supported by high net margin of 0.364 and ROE of 0.34. However, cash flow metrics are unavailable, so cash conversion cannot be assessed.  
   - **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Debt-to-equity is low at 0.177. Net cash (cash minus debt) is $35.11B. Equity growth indicates retained earnings strength, with total liabilities of $231.12B covered by assets of $484.27B.

3. **Valuation versus the Company's Own Supplied History**  
   - Latest PE diluted of 38.751 is near the high end of the 31.7–39.7 range over the past year (2023-07-03 to 2024-06-28).  
   - EV/EBIT of 31.245 is at the upper bound of the 25.0–31.7 range.  
   - P/B of 13.192 is within the 11.1–13.9 range but elevated relative to history.  
   - Earnings yield is low at 0.026, reflecting high valuation multiples.

4. **Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context versus Fundamental Path**  
   - **Price:** Latest close of $446.95 (2024-06-28). Annual return of +33.1% (from $335.85), quarterly return of +6.2% (from $420.72).  
   - **Fundamentals:** YoY earnings growth of +20.0% and revenue growth of +17.0% suggest price growth has outpaced earnings, indicating multiple expansion. QoQ revenue decline of -0.3% contrasts with the +6.2% quarterly price increase.

5. **Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**  
   - **Upside:** Consistent earnings growth (YoY EPS +20.0%), high margins (gross 0.699), strong balance sheet with net cash of $35.11B and low debt-to-equity of 0.177.  
   - **Downside:** Elevated valuation (PE 38.751 at historical highs), price appreciation exceeding fundamental growth, QoQ revenue dip of -0.3%, and unverified cash flows.

6. **Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**  
   - Fundamentally, MSFT exhibits robust growth, high profitability, and balance-sheet resilience. However, valuation multiples are stretched relative to historical ranges, with the stock trading near the high end of PE and EV/EBIT bands. The risk is that current multiples may not sustain if growth decelerates, as hinted by QoQ revenue softness. Reward hinges on continued execution of earnings growth to justify valuation.
MSFT.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: MSFT**
**Decision Date: 2024-07-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read from Narrative**
Based on the most recent annual filing (10-K, period ending 2023-06-30), Microsoft's franchise is defined as a technology company focused on empowering productivity and business processes, building the intelligent cloud and edge platform, and creating more personal computing. The company's strategy is heavily centered on infusing artificial intelligence (AI) across its entire portfolio, including Microsoft 365 Copilot, Azure AI, GitHub Copilot, and Bing. The business is structured around three segments: Productivity and Business Processes (Office, LinkedIn, Dynamics), Intelligent Cloud (Azure, server products, Nuance), and More Personal Computing (Windows, Devices, Gaming). A key strategic asset is the long-term partnership with OpenAI, where Azure is the exclusive cloud provider for OpenAI's workloads.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated, from Supplied Text)**
*   **AI Leadership & Investor Sentiment (Multiple dates in June 2024):** Consistent media narrative (e.g., 2024-06-30, 2024-06-28) identifies Microsoft as a top AI stock and a "must-own" for growth investors, citing its robust presence in high-growth industries and expanding cloud platform.
*   **Regulatory Risk Materialization (2024-06-25 to 2024-06-28):** The European Commission (EC) formally accused Microsoft of antitrust violations related to bundling its Teams application with Office 365 and Microsoft 365 suites (Bloomberg, Reuters, Arstechnica). The EC stated that remedial changes made by Microsoft were insufficient and that the conduct "may have prevented Teams’ rivals from competing." Microsoft faces a potential fine of up to 10% of its global annual turnover.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Escalating Regulatory Pressure.** The EU antitrust charge (2024-06-25) over Teams bundling is a confirmed, active legal risk. The precedent of significant prior EU fines (€2.2 billion in the previous decade) and the potential penalty scale (10% of global revenue) create material financial and operational risk. The case could force unwinding of profitable product integrations or restrict future bundling strategies.
*   **Offset: Dominant Market Position & Execution.** The 10-K and recent market narrative describe Microsoft's entrenched position in enterprise productivity software and cloud infrastructure (Azure). Its integrated "Microsoft Cloud" stack and massive installed base provide a durable competitive advantage and pricing power that are difficult for regulators to dismantle quickly.
*   **Offset: Strong AI Narrative and Catalysts.** Recent media (June 2024) consistently frames Microsoft as a leader in the AI investment cycle. The company's stated strategy of "infusing generative AI capability into our consumer and commercial offerings" (10-K) and its exclusive cloud partnership with OpenAI position it to potentially capture a disproportionate share of AI infrastructure and application spending.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Positive.** Microsoft's franchise exhibits strong qualitative characteristics anchored in AI leadership and deep enterprise integration. The recent narrative is overwhelmingly positive, reinforcing its position as a core AI beneficiary. The primary qualitative risk—a significant EU antitrust enforcement action over Teams—is material but is partially offset by the company's market dominance and the powerful secular tailwind of AI adoption. The balance of evidence points to continued strategic momentum, albeit with a notable and active regulatory overhang.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Microsoft's fundamental engine remains exceptional—accelerating revenue growth (+17% YoY), expanding margins (net margin 36.4%), and a fortress balance sheet ($35.1B net cash)—but the stock is priced for perfection at a P/E of 38.8x (near the top of its 1-year range), leaving little margin of safety. The EU antitrust action over Teams bundling introduces a new, material regulatory overhang that partially offsets the powerful AI tailwind.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Microsoft's fundamental engine remains exceptional—accelerating revenue growth (+17% YoY), expanding margins (net margin 36.4%), and a fortress balance sheet ($35.1B net cash)—but the stock is priced for perfection at a P/E of 38.8x (near the top of its 1-year range), leaving little margin of safety. The EU antitrust action over Teams bundling introduces a new, material regulatory overhang that partially offsets the powerful AI tailwind.
key_factors: Robust fundamental growth: Q3 FY2024 revenue grew +17.0% YoY to $61.86B, with EPS diluted up +20.0% YoY to $2.94, reflecting accelerating Azure AI demand and strong enterprise adoption.

Valuation at historical extremes: P/E of 38.8x is near the top of its 1-year range (31.7–39.7), EV/EBIT of 31.2x at the upper bound of its 25.0–31.7 range, and earnings yield of just 2.6%—leaving minimal margin of safety for any growth deceleration.

EU antitrust enforcement action: The European Commission formally accused Microsoft on 2024-06-25 of antitrust violations over Teams bundling with Office 365, with potential fines up to 10% of global annual turnover and risk of forced product unbundling.

Balance-sheet resilience: Net cash of $35.1B, debt-to-equity of 0.177, and equity growth to $253.1B provide a strong buffer against macro or regulatory shocks, supporting continued investment in AI infrastructure.
